1. 1 Introduction
    1. 1.1 Notation and Terminology
  2. 2 Known Results and Lemmas
  3. 3 Proof of Conjecture 1
  4. 4 Conclusion

Another conjecture of TxGraffiti concerning
zero forcing and domination in graphs

1,2Randy Davila 
1Research and Development
RelationalAI
Berkeley, CA 94704, USA
Email: randy.davila@relational.ai

2Department of Computational Applied
Mathematics & Operations Research
Rice University
Houston, TX 77005, USA
Email: randy.r.davila@rice.edu

Abstract

This paper proves a conjecture generated by the artificial intelligence conjecturing program called TxGraffiti. More specifically, we show that if G𝐺Gitalic_G is a connected, cubic, and claw-free graph, then Z⁢(G)≤γ⁢(G)+2𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺2Z(G)\leq\gamma(G)+2italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2, where Z⁢(G)𝑍𝐺Z(G)italic_Z ( italic_G ) and γ⁢(G)𝛾𝐺\gamma(G)italic_γ ( italic_G ) denote the zero forcing number and the domination number of G𝐺Gitalic_G, respectively. Furthermore, we provide a complete characterization of graphs that achieve this bound. Notably, this bound improves the known upper bounds for the zero forcing number of connected, cubic, and claw-free graphs.

Keywords: Automated conjecturing; domination number; TxGraffiti; zero forcing number.
AMS subject classification: 05C69

1 Introduction

Written by the author in 2017 and tailored towards graph theoretic conjectures, TxGraffiti is a machine learning and artificially intelligent program for generating mathematical conjectures suitable for research by professional mathematicians; now available as an interactive website111https://txgraffiti.streamlit.app. The name TxGraffiti plays a homage to both the original conjecturing program Graffiti written by Fajtlowicz [12], and its successor program Graffiti.pc written by DeLaViña [11]. However, the design of TxGraffiti remains distinct from both Graffiti and Graffiti.pc, so the conjectures of TxGraffiti differ in many instances. One particularly interesting difference is the ability of TxGraffiti to make particularly strong and interesting conjectures on domination and zero forcing-related parameters for cubic graphs. One such example is that if G≠K4𝐺subscript𝐾4G\neq K_{4}italic_G ≠ italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 4 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is a connected and cubic graph, then Z⁢(G)≤2⁢γ⁢(G)𝑍𝐺2𝛾𝐺Z(G)\leq 2\gamma(G)italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ 2 italic_γ ( italic_G ), where Z⁢(G)𝑍𝐺Z(G)italic_Z ( italic_G ) and γ⁢(G)𝛾𝐺\gamma(G)italic_γ ( italic_G ) denote the zero forcing number and domination number of G𝐺Gitalic_G, respectively, a conjecture that was confirmed in [6, 9]. Notably, a similar conjecture of TxGraffiti relating total zero forcing and total domination for cubic graphs was also shown to be true in [6, 8]. A more recent conjecture on zero-forcing made by TxGraffiti states that the zero forcing number is, at most, the vertex cover number for claw-free graphs, which in turn was confirmed and led to a larger body of work presented by Brimkov et al. [3]. For TxGraffiti inspired results that do not concern the zero forcing number of a graph, see [4], or see the surprising result given by Caro et al. [5], which states that α⁢(G)≤μ⁢(G)𝛼𝐺𝜇𝐺\alpha(G)\leq\mu(G)italic_α ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_μ ( italic_G ) for any regular graph G𝐺Gitalic_G, where α⁢(G)𝛼𝐺\alpha(G)italic_α ( italic_G ) and μ⁢(G)𝜇𝐺\mu(G)italic_μ ( italic_G ) denote the independence number and matching number of G𝐺Gitalic_G, respectively.

Though many conjectures of TxGraffiti are confirmed, many more remain open, and even more remain undiscovered. The most well-known of these open conjectures is likely the α𝛼\alphaitalic_α-Z Conjecture; namely, if G≠K4𝐺subscript𝐾4G\neq K_{4}italic_G ≠ italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 4 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is a connected and cubic graph, then Z⁢(G)≤α⁢(G)+1𝑍𝐺𝛼𝐺1Z(G)\leq\alpha(G)+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_α ( italic_G ) + 1; see the last page of Davila’s dissertation [6]. The α𝛼\alphaitalic_α-Z conjecture has received a considerable amount of attention with several partial results, including Z⁢(G)≤α⁢(G)+1𝑍𝐺𝛼𝐺1Z(G)\leq\alpha(G)+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_α ( italic_G ) + 1, whenever G≠K4𝐺subscript𝐾4G\neq K_{4}italic_G ≠ italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 4 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is connected, cubic, and claw-free [6, 10]. Notably, this partial result was not conjectured by TxGraffiti, and so, by the design of TxGraffiti, there must exist a more substantial upper bound for Z⁢(G)𝑍𝐺Z(G)italic_Z ( italic_G ) in cubic and claw-free graphs in terms of a graph parameter other than α⁢(G)𝛼𝐺\alpha(G)italic_α ( italic_G ). Thus, we discovered the following stronger conjecture when explicitly examining the conjectures of TxGraffiti for connected, cubic, and claw-free graphs.

Conjecture 1 (TxGraffiti – confirmed).

If G𝐺Gitalic_G is a connected, cubic, and claw-free graph, then

Z⁢(G)≤γ⁢(G)+2,𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺2Z(G)\leq\gamma(G)+2,italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2 ,

and this bound is sharp.

In this paper, we resolve Conjecture 1 in the affirmative, and in doing so, also characterize graphs attaining equality in its statement. Notably, the resulting theorem improves on both Z⁢(G)≤α⁢(G)+1𝑍𝐺𝛼𝐺1Z(G)\leq\alpha(G)+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_α ( italic_G ) + 1 and Z⁢(G)≤2⁢γ⁢(G)𝑍𝐺2𝛾𝐺Z(G)\leq 2\gamma(G)italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ 2 italic_γ ( italic_G ) (for G≠K4𝐺subscript𝐾4G\neq K_{4}italic_G ≠ italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 4 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT) whenever G𝐺Gitalic_G is a connected, cubic, and claw-free graph.

1.1 Notation and Terminology

Throughout this paper, all graphs considered are simple, undirected, and finite. Let G𝐺Gitalic_G be a graph with vertex set V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) and edge set E⁢(G)𝐸𝐺E(G)italic_E ( italic_G ). The order of G𝐺Gitalic_G is n⁢(G)=|V⁢(G)|𝑛𝐺𝑉𝐺n(G)=|V(G)|italic_n ( italic_G ) = | italic_V ( italic_G ) |. Two vertices v,w∈V⁢(G)𝑣𝑤𝑉𝐺v,w\in V(G)italic_v , italic_w ∈ italic_V ( italic_G ) are neighbors, or adjacent, if v⁢w∈E⁢(G)𝑣𝑤𝐸𝐺vw\in E(G)italic_v italic_w ∈ italic_E ( italic_G ). The open neighborhood of v∈V⁢(G)𝑣𝑉𝐺v\in V(G)italic_v ∈ italic_V ( italic_G ), is the set of neighbors of v𝑣vitalic_v, denoted NG⁢(v)subscript𝑁𝐺𝑣N_{G}(v)italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_v ). The closed neighborhood of S⊆V𝑆𝑉S\subseteq Vitalic_S ⊆ italic_V is NG⁢[S]=⋃v∈SNG⁢[v]subscript𝑁𝐺delimited-[]𝑆subscript𝑣𝑆subscript𝑁𝐺delimited-[]𝑣N_{G}[S]=\bigcup_{v\in S}N_{G}[v]italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT [ italic_S ] = ⋃ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v ∈ italic_S end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT [ italic_v ]. The degree of a vertex v∈V⁢(G)𝑣𝑉𝐺v\in V(G)italic_v ∈ italic_V ( italic_G ), denoted dG⁢(v)subscript𝑑𝐺𝑣d_{G}(v)italic_d start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_v ), is equal to |NG⁢(v)|subscript𝑁𝐺𝑣|N_{G}(v)|| italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_v ) |. A cubic graph (also called a 3-regular graph) is a graph for which every vertex degree is three. The maximum and minimum degree of G𝐺Gitalic_G will be denoted Δ⁢(G)Δ𝐺\Delta(G)roman_Δ ( italic_G ) and δ⁢(G)𝛿𝐺\delta(G)italic_δ ( italic_G ), respectively. When there is no scope for confusion, we will use the notation n=n⁢(G)𝑛𝑛𝐺n=n(G)italic_n = italic_n ( italic_G ), δ=δ⁢(G)𝛿𝛿𝐺\delta=\delta(G)italic_δ = italic_δ ( italic_G ), and Δ=Δ⁢(G)ΔΔ𝐺\Delta=\Delta(G)roman_Δ = roman_Δ ( italic_G ), to denote the order, minimum degree, and maximum degree, respectively.

Two vertices in a graph G𝐺Gitalic_G are independent if they are not neighbors. A set of pairwise independent vertices in G𝐺Gitalic_G is an independent set of G𝐺Gitalic_G. The number of vertices in a maximum independent set in G𝐺Gitalic_G is the independence number of G𝐺Gitalic_G, denoted α⁢(G)𝛼𝐺\alpha(G)italic_α ( italic_G ). A dominating set in G𝐺Gitalic_G is a subset X⊆V⁢(G)𝑋𝑉𝐺X\subseteq V(G)italic_X ⊆ italic_V ( italic_G ), so all vertices in G𝐺Gitalic_G are either in X𝑋Xitalic_X or adjacent with at least one vertex in X𝑋Xitalic_X. The domination number of G𝐺Gitalic_G, denoted by γ⁢(G)𝛾𝐺\gamma(G)italic_γ ( italic_G ), is the cardinality of a minimum dominating set in G𝐺Gitalic_G. If X⊆V⁢(G)𝑋𝑉𝐺X\subseteq V(G)italic_X ⊆ italic_V ( italic_G ) is a dominating and independent set, then X𝑋Xitalic_X is an independent dominating set. The minimum cardinality of an independent dominating set in G𝐺Gitalic_G is the independent domination number of G𝐺Gitalic_G, denoted i⁢(G)𝑖𝐺i(G)italic_i ( italic_G ). For more on domination in graphs, see the comprehensive and well-written text by Haynes, Hedetniemi, and Henning [16].

For a set of vertices S⊆V⁢(G)𝑆𝑉𝐺S\subseteq V(G)italic_S ⊆ italic_V ( italic_G ), the subgraph induced by S𝑆Sitalic_S is denoted by G⁢[S]𝐺delimited-[]𝑆G[S]italic_G [ italic_S ]. If v∈V⁢(G)𝑣𝑉𝐺v\in V(G)italic_v ∈ italic_V ( italic_G ), we denote the graph obtained by deleting v𝑣vitalic_v in G𝐺Gitalic_G by G−v𝐺𝑣G-vitalic_G - italic_v. We denote the path, cycle, and complete graph on n𝑛nitalic_n vertices by Pnsubscript𝑃𝑛P_{n}italic_P start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, Cnsubscript𝐶𝑛C_{n}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and K4subscript𝐾4K_{4}italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 4 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, respectively. A triangle in G𝐺Gitalic_G is an induced subgraph of G𝐺Gitalic_G isomorphic to K3subscript𝐾3K_{3}italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 3 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, whereas a diamond in G𝐺Gitalic_G is a subgraph of G𝐺Gitalic_G isomorphic to K4subscript𝐾4K_{4}italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 4 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT with one edge missing. A graph G𝐺Gitalic_G is F𝐹Fitalic_F-free if G𝐺Gitalic_G does not contain F𝐹Fitalic_F as an induced subgraph. In particular, if G𝐺Gitalic_G is F𝐹Fitalic_F-free, where F=K1,3𝐹subscript𝐾13F=K_{1,3}italic_F = italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 , 3 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, then G𝐺Gitalic_G is claw-free. Claw-free graphs have been widely studied, and the survey by Flandrin, Faudree, and Ryjáček [14] is the standard reference for the topic.

Let S⊆V⁢(G)𝑆𝑉𝐺S\subseteq V(G)italic_S ⊆ italic_V ( italic_G ) be a set of initially “blue-colored” vertices, all remaining vertices being “white-colored”. The zero forcing process on G𝐺Gitalic_G is defined as follows: At each discrete time step, if a blue-colored vertex has a unique white-colored neighbor, then this blue-colored vertex forces its white-colored neighbor to become colored blue. The open z𝑧zitalic_z-neighborhood of S𝑆Sitalic_S in G𝐺Gitalic_G, written NGz⁢(S)superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧𝑆N_{G}^{z}(S)italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_S ), is the set of all initially white-colored vertices that change color during the zero forcing process. The closed z𝑧zitalic_z-neighborhood of S𝑆Sitalic_S in G𝐺Gitalic_G is the set NGz⁢[S]=S∪NGz⁢(S)superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]𝑆𝑆superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧𝑆N_{G}^{z}[S]=S\cup N_{G}^{z}(S)italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S ] = italic_S ∪ italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( italic_S ). A valid-z𝑧zitalic_z-extension rule on S𝑆Sitalic_S is a mapping from S𝑆Sitalic_S to a new set S′superscript𝑆′S^{\prime}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, such that |S|=|S′|𝑆superscript𝑆′|S|=|S^{\prime}|| italic_S | = | italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | and NGz⁢[S]⊆NGz⁢[S′]superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]𝑆superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]superscript𝑆′N_{G}^{z}[S]\subseteq N_{G}^{z}[S^{\prime}]italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S ] ⊆ italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ]; if in addition NGz⁢[S]⊂NGz⁢[S′]superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]𝑆superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]superscript𝑆′N_{G}^{z}[S]\subset N_{G}^{z}[S^{\prime}]italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S ] ⊂ italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ], then we say the rule is a valid-z𝑧zitalic_z-proper-extension rule on S𝑆Sitalic_S. A set X⊆V⁢(G)𝑋𝑉𝐺X\subseteq V(G)italic_X ⊆ italic_V ( italic_G ) is S𝑆Sitalic_S-reachable when X⊆NGz⁢[S]𝑋superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]𝑆X\subseteq N_{G}^{z}[S]italic_X ⊆ italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S ]. If V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) is S𝑆Sitalic_S-reachable, and so, V⁢(G)=NGz⁢[S]𝑉𝐺superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]𝑆V(G)=N_{G}^{z}[S]italic_V ( italic_G ) = italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S ], then S𝑆Sitalic_S is a zero forcing set of G𝐺Gitalic_G. The minimum cardinality of a zero forcing set in G𝐺Gitalic_G is the zero forcing number of G𝐺Gitalic_G. Zero forcing was introduced in [1] in the context of linear algebra and has since gained much interest in graph theory. For more information on zero forcing in graphs, we refer the reader to the excellent monograph by Hogben, Lin, and Shader [13].

For notation and graph terminology not mentioned here, we refer the reader to [16]. We also will use the standard notation [k]={1,…,k}delimited-[]𝑘1…𝑘[k]=\{1,\ldots,k\}[ italic_k ] = { 1 , … , italic_k }.

2 Known Results and Lemmas

In this section, we introduce terminology, recall useful results, and prove technical lemmas needed for the proof of Conjecture 1. To begin, recall the following result of Allan and Laskar [2], which states that the independent domination number equals the domination number for all claw-free graphs.

Theorem 2 ([2]).

If G𝐺Gitalic_G is a claw-free graph, then i⁢(G)=γ⁢(G)𝑖𝐺𝛾𝐺i(G)=\gamma(G)italic_i ( italic_G ) = italic_γ ( italic_G ).

We will also need the following structural property of connected, cubic, and claw-free graphs established by Henning and Löwenstein in [17].

Lemma 3 ([17]).

If G≠K4𝐺subscript𝐾4G\neq K_{4}italic_G ≠ italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 4 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is a connected, cubic, and claw-free graph, then the vertex set V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) can be uniquely partitioned into sets, each of which induces a triangle or diamond in G𝐺Gitalic_G.

By Lemma 3, the vertex set V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) of a connected, cubic, and claw-free graph G≠K4𝐺subscript𝐾4G\neq K_{4}italic_G ≠ italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 4 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT can be uniquely partitioned into sets, each of which induces either a triangle subgraph or a diamond subgraph of G𝐺Gitalic_G. We refer to such a partition of G𝐺Gitalic_G as a triangle-diamond partition of G𝐺Gitalic_G, abbreviated ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition. Borrowing the terminology introduced in [17], we call every triangle and diamond induced by our ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition a unit of the partition. A unit that is a triangle is called a triangle-unit and a unit that is a diamond is called a diamond-unit. Note that triangle-units do not belong to any diamond-unit. Furthermore, we say that two units are adjacent if an edge joins a vertex in one unit to a vertex in another and that a vertex is adjacent to a unit whenever the vertex has a neighbor.

Henning and Löwenstein also introduced the notation and definition of a diamond-necklace, which is now restated. For k≥2𝑘2k\geq 2italic_k ≥ 2 an integer, let Nksubscript𝑁𝑘N_{k}italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be the connected and cubic graph constructed as follows. Take k𝑘kitalic_k disjoint copies D1,D2,…,Dksubscript𝐷1subscript𝐷2…subscript𝐷𝑘D_{1},D_{2},...,D_{k}italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , … , italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT of a diamond, where V⁢(Di)={ai,bi,ci,di}𝑉subscript𝐷𝑖subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑐𝑖subscript𝑑𝑖V(D_{i})=\{a_{i},b_{i},c_{i},d_{i}\}italic_V ( italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) = { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_d start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } and where ai⁢bisubscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑏𝑖a_{i}b_{i}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is the missing edge in Disubscript𝐷𝑖D_{i}italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Let Nksubscript𝑁𝑘N_{k}italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be obtained from the disjoint union of these k𝑘kitalic_k diamonds by adding the edges {ai⁢bi+1|i∈[k−1]}conditional-setsubscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑏𝑖1𝑖delimited-[]𝑘1\{a_{i}b_{i+1}\>|\>i\in[k-1]\}{ italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i + 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_i ∈ [ italic_k - 1 ] } and adding the edge ak⁢b1subscript𝑎𝑘subscript𝑏1a_{k}b_{1}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. We call Nksubscript𝑁𝑘N_{k}italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT a diamond-necklace with k𝑘kitalic_k diamonds; see Figure 1 for the diamond-necklace N4subscript𝑁4N_{4}italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 4 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Let 𝒩c⁢u⁢b⁢i⁢c={Nk|k≥2}subscript𝒩𝑐𝑢𝑏𝑖𝑐conditional-setsubscript𝑁𝑘𝑘2\mathcal{N}_{cubic}=\{N_{k}\>|\>k\geq 2\}caligraphic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_c italic_u italic_b italic_i italic_c end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = { italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_k ≥ 2 }. The complete graph K4subscript𝐾4K_{4}italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 4 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT may actually be thought of as a loop diamond-necklace, and so, we define 𝒩c⁢u⁢b⁢i⁢c∗=𝒩c⁢u⁢b⁢i⁢c∪{K4}superscriptsubscript𝒩𝑐𝑢𝑏𝑖𝑐subscript𝒩𝑐𝑢𝑏𝑖𝑐subscript𝐾4\mathcal{N}_{cubic}^{*}=\mathcal{N}_{cubic}\cup\{K_{4}\}caligraphic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_c italic_u italic_b italic_i italic_c end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = caligraphic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_c italic_u italic_b italic_i italic_c end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ∪ { italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 4 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT }. In [6, 7], it was established that if G∈𝒩c⁢u⁢b⁢i⁢c𝐺subscript𝒩𝑐𝑢𝑏𝑖𝑐G\in\mathcal{N}_{cubic}italic_G ∈ caligraphic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_c italic_u italic_b italic_i italic_c end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, then Z⁢(G)=14⁢n⁢(G)+2𝑍𝐺14𝑛𝐺2Z(G)=\frac{1}{4}n(G)+2italic_Z ( italic_G ) = divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG 4 end_ARG italic_n ( italic_G ) + 2. This together with the fact that Z⁢(K4)=3=14⁢n⁢(K4)+2𝑍subscript𝐾4314𝑛subscript𝐾42Z(K_{4})=3=\frac{1}{4}n(K_{4})+2italic_Z ( italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 4 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) = 3 = divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG 4 end_ARG italic_n ( italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 4 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) + 2, imply the following lemma.

Lemma 4.

If G∈𝒩c⁢u⁢b⁢i⁢c∗𝐺superscriptsubscript𝒩𝑐𝑢𝑏𝑖𝑐G\in\mathcal{N}_{cubic}^{*}italic_G ∈ caligraphic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_c italic_u italic_b italic_i italic_c end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT has order n𝑛nitalic_n, then Z⁢(G)=14⁢n+2𝑍𝐺14𝑛2Z(G)=\frac{1}{4}n+2italic_Z ( italic_G ) = divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG 4 end_ARG italic_n + 2.

Let D𝐷Ditalic_D be a diamond-unit in the ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition of a cubic and claw-free graph G𝐺Gitalic_G and let V⁢(D)={a,b,c,d}𝑉𝐷𝑎𝑏𝑐𝑑V(D)=\{a,b,c,d\}italic_V ( italic_D ) = { italic_a , italic_b , italic_c , italic_d } denote the vertex set of D𝐷Ditalic_D, where a⁢b𝑎𝑏abitalic_a italic_b is the missing edge in D𝐷Ditalic_D. Then, the only neighbors of vertices b𝑏bitalic_b and c𝑐citalic_c, are vertices contained in V⁢(D)𝑉𝐷V(D)italic_V ( italic_D ), and as a consequence of this, we obtain the following lemma.

Lemma 5.

If D𝐷Ditalic_D is a diamond-unit in a ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition of the cubic and claw-free graph G𝐺Gitalic_G, and if X⊆V⁢(G)𝑋𝑉𝐺X\subseteq V(G)italic_X ⊆ italic_V ( italic_G ) is a dominating set of G𝐺Gitalic_G, then V⁢(D)∩X≠∅𝑉𝐷𝑋V(D)\cap X\neq\emptysetitalic_V ( italic_D ) ∩ italic_X ≠ ∅.

A trivial lower bound for the domination number of any graph G𝐺Gitalic_G of order n𝑛nitalic_n and maximum degree ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ, is γ⁢(G)≥nΔ+1𝛾𝐺𝑛Δ1\gamma(G)\geq\frac{n}{\Delta+1}italic_γ ( italic_G ) ≥ divide start_ARG italic_n end_ARG start_ARG roman_Δ + 1 end_ARG. Thus, for a cubic graph G𝐺Gitalic_G of order n𝑛nitalic_n, we have the lower bound γ⁢(G)≥n4𝛾𝐺𝑛4\gamma(G)\geq\frac{n}{4}italic_γ ( italic_G ) ≥ divide start_ARG italic_n end_ARG start_ARG 4 end_ARG. Let D1,D2,…,Dksubscript𝐷1subscript𝐷2…subscript𝐷𝑘D_{1},D_{2},...,D_{k}italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , … , italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be the diamond-units in the diamond-necklace Nksubscript𝑁𝑘N_{k}italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, where V⁢(Di)={ai,bi,ci,di}𝑉subscript𝐷𝑖subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑐𝑖subscript𝑑𝑖V(D_{i})=\{a_{i},b_{i},c_{i},d_{i}\}italic_V ( italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) = { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_d start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } and where ai⁢bisubscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑏𝑖a_{i}b_{i}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is the missing edge in Disubscript𝐷𝑖D_{i}italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and let n𝑛nitalic_n be the order of Nksubscript𝑁𝑘N_{k}italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. It is easy to see that X={c1,…,ck}𝑋subscript𝑐1…subscript𝑐𝑘X=\{c_{1},\dots,c_{k}\}italic_X = { italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , … , italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } is dominating set of Nksubscript𝑁𝑘N_{k}italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, where |X|=n4𝑋𝑛4|X|=\frac{n}{4}| italic_X | = divide start_ARG italic_n end_ARG start_ARG 4 end_ARG. This, together with the lower bound γ⁢(Nk)≥n4𝛾subscript𝑁𝑘𝑛4\gamma(N_{k})\geq\frac{n}{4}italic_γ ( italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) ≥ divide start_ARG italic_n end_ARG start_ARG 4 end_ARG, imply that X𝑋Xitalic_X is a minimum dominating set for Nksubscript𝑁𝑘N_{k}italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and so, γ⁢(Nk)=n4𝛾subscript𝑁𝑘𝑛4\gamma(N_{k})=\frac{n}{4}italic_γ ( italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) = divide start_ARG italic_n end_ARG start_ARG 4 end_ARG. This result can be combined with Lemma 4, implying the following formula for the zero forcing number of diamond-necklaces in terms of the domination number.

Lemma 6.

If G∈𝒩c⁢u⁢b⁢i⁢c∗𝐺superscriptsubscript𝒩𝑐𝑢𝑏𝑖𝑐G\in\mathcal{N}_{cubic}^{*}italic_G ∈ caligraphic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_c italic_u italic_b italic_i italic_c end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, then Z⁢(G)=γ⁢(G)+2𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺2Z(G)=\gamma(G)+2italic_Z ( italic_G ) = italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2.

The following result improves on the statement of Conjecture 1 when the graph G𝐺Gitalic_G has no diamond-unit in its ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition.

Lemma 7.

If G𝐺Gitalic_G is a connected and cubic graph with a spanning 2-factor consisting only of triangles, then Z⁢(G)≤γ⁢(G)+1𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺1Z(G)\leq\gamma(G)+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 1.

Proof.

Let G𝐺Gitalic_G be a connected and cubic graph with a spanning 2-factor consisting only of triangles. Thus, G𝐺Gitalic_G is connected, cubic, and claw-free, where every unit in the ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition of V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) is a triangle-unit. Thus, G𝐺Gitalic_G is diamond-free. We next define the contraction multigraph of G𝐺Gitalic_G, denoted MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, to be the multigraph whose vertices correspond to the triangle-units in the ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition of G𝐺Gitalic_G, and where two vertices of MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT are joined by the number of edges joining the corresponding triangle-units in G𝐺Gitalic_G. By construction, MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT has no loops but does possibly contain multiedges. Note that n⁢(MG)𝑛subscript𝑀𝐺n(M_{G})italic_n ( italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) is precisely the number of triangle-units in G𝐺Gitalic_G. Since G𝐺Gitalic_G contains at least two triangle-units, it must be the case that n⁢(MG)≥2𝑛subscript𝑀𝐺2n(M_{G})\geq 2italic_n ( italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) ≥ 2. Moreover, each vertex of MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT has degree three, and so, MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is a cubic multigraph. For each v∈V⁢(MG)𝑣𝑉subscript𝑀𝐺v\in V(M_{G})italic_v ∈ italic_V ( italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ), let V⁢(Tv)={av,bv,cv}𝑉subscript𝑇𝑣subscript𝑎𝑣subscript𝑏𝑣subscript𝑐𝑣V(T_{v})=\{a_{v},b_{v},c_{v}\}italic_V ( italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) = { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } denote the vertices of the triangle-unit in G𝐺Gitalic_G associated with v𝑣vitalic_v. Further, we say that Tvsubscript𝑇𝑣T_{v}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is derived from v𝑣vitalic_v in MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT.

Let X⊆V⁢(G)𝑋𝑉𝐺X\subseteq V(G)italic_X ⊆ italic_V ( italic_G ) be a minimum (independent) dominating set of G𝐺Gitalic_G and let Y=V⁢(G)∖X𝑌𝑉𝐺𝑋Y=V(G)\setminus Xitalic_Y = italic_V ( italic_G ) ∖ italic_X. Thus, every vertex of G𝐺Gitalic_G not in X𝑋Xitalic_X has a neighbor in X𝑋Xitalic_X, and no two vertices in X𝑋Xitalic_X are endpoints of the same edge in G𝐺Gitalic_G; also, |X|=γ⁢(G)𝑋𝛾𝐺|X|=\gamma(G)| italic_X | = italic_γ ( italic_G ). Further note that if T𝑇Titalic_T is a triangle-unit in the ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition of V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ), then either T𝑇Titalic_T contains precisely one vertex from X𝑋Xitalic_X (recall X𝑋Xitalic_X is an independent set), or each of the triangle-units adjacent with Tvsubscript𝑇𝑣T_{v}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT contains a vertex from X𝑋Xitalic_X. Since G𝐺Gitalic_G is a cubic graph and every vertex not in X𝑋Xitalic_X has a neighbor in X𝑋Xitalic_X, next observe Δ⁢(G⁢[Y])=2Δ𝐺delimited-[]𝑌2\Delta(G[Y])=2roman_Δ ( italic_G [ italic_Y ] ) = 2. Therefore, G⁢[Y]𝐺delimited-[]𝑌G[Y]italic_G [ italic_Y ] is either a path, a cycle, or a disjoint union of paths and cycles. In the following two initialization procedures, we define how to color a set of blue vertices using the dominating set X𝑋Xitalic_X, from which we construct a zero forcing set of G𝐺Gitalic_G.

Path Initialization. If G⁢[Y]𝐺delimited-[]𝑌G[Y]italic_G [ italic_Y ] contains at least one path component, let

HG:bv1⁢av2⁢…⁢bvℓ⁢cvℓ:subscript𝐻𝐺subscript𝑏subscript𝑣1subscript𝑎subscript𝑣2…subscript𝑏subscript𝑣ℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑣ℓH_{G}:b_{v_{1}}a_{v_{2}}\dots b_{v_{\ell}}c_{v_{\ell}}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT : italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT … italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT

be a maximum path component of G⁢[Y]𝐺delimited-[]𝑌G[Y]italic_G [ italic_Y ], and note that HGsubscript𝐻𝐺H_{G}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT corresponds to the path

HM:v1,v2,…,vℓ,:subscript𝐻𝑀subscript𝑣1subscript𝑣2…subscript𝑣ℓH_{M}:v_{1},v_{2},\dots,v_{\ell},italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_M end_POSTSUBSCRIPT : italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , … , italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ,

in the multigraph MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Next, color blue the vertices in S=X∪{bv1}𝑆𝑋subscript𝑏subscript𝑣1S=X\cup\{b_{v_{1}}\}italic_S = italic_X ∪ { italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } and color white the vertices in V⁢(G)∖S𝑉𝐺𝑆V(G)\setminus Sitalic_V ( italic_G ) ∖ italic_S. Under this coloring of the vertices in V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ), observe that the only white-colored neighbor of bv1subscript𝑏subscript𝑣1b_{v_{1}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is cv1subscript𝑐subscript𝑣1c_{v_{1}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and so, the zero forcing process may begin by bv1subscript𝑏subscript𝑣1b_{v_{1}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT forcing cv1subscript𝑐subscript𝑣1c_{v_{1}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become colored blue. After cv1subscript𝑐subscript𝑣1c_{v_{1}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT becomes colored blue, then the only possibly white-colored neighbor of cv1subscript𝑐subscript𝑣1c_{v_{1}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would be bv2subscript𝑏subscript𝑣2b_{v_{2}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and so, cv1subscript𝑐subscript𝑣1c_{v_{1}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT may then bv2subscript𝑏subscript𝑣2b_{v_{2}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become colored blue. This process continues until bvℓsubscript𝑏subscript𝑣ℓb_{v_{\ell}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT become colored blue, and thereafter forces cvℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑣ℓc_{v_{\ell}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become colored blue; see Figure 2. Thus, all vertices in the path component HGsubscript𝐻𝐺H_{G}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT become colored blue, and more so, the derived triangle-units from the vertices of HMsubscript𝐻𝑀H_{M}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_M end_POSTSUBSCRIPT contain only blue-colored vertices. If G⁢[Y]𝐺delimited-[]𝑌G[Y]italic_G [ italic_Y ] does not contain a path component, move to the next initialization procedure.

Cycle Initialization. If G⁢[Y]𝐺delimited-[]𝑌G[Y]italic_G [ italic_Y ] does not contain a path component, then let

HG:bv1⁢av2⁢…⁢bvℓ⁢cvℓ⁢bv1,:subscript𝐻𝐺subscript𝑏subscript𝑣1subscript𝑎subscript𝑣2…subscript𝑏subscript𝑣ℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑣ℓsubscript𝑏subscript𝑣1H_{G}:b_{v_{1}}a_{v_{2}}\dots b_{v_{\ell}}c_{v_{\ell}b_{v_{1}}},italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT : italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT … italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ,

be a largest cycle component of G⁢[Y]𝐺delimited-[]𝑌G[Y]italic_G [ italic_Y ], where

HM:v1⁢v2⁢…⁢vℓ⁢v1,:subscript𝐻𝑀subscript𝑣1subscript𝑣2…subscript𝑣ℓsubscript𝑣1H_{M}:v_{1}v_{2}\dots v_{\ell}v_{1},italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_M end_POSTSUBSCRIPT : italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT … italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ,

is a corresponding cycle (allowing 2-cycles) in the multigraph MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Since G𝐺Gitalic_G is cubic, claw-free, and diamond-free, a vertex from the dominating set X𝑋Xitalic_X of G𝐺Gitalic_G will dominate at most two vertices on the cycle component HGsubscript𝐻𝐺H_{G}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and if so, then these two vertices must be neighbors on the component HGsubscript𝐻𝐺H_{G}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Moreover, if a vertex of w∈X𝑤𝑋w\in Xitalic_w ∈ italic_X dominates exactly one vertex in HGsubscript𝐻𝐺H_{G}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, say b∈V⁢(H)𝑏𝑉𝐻b\in V(H)italic_b ∈ italic_V ( italic_H ), then H𝐻Hitalic_H is the cycle C3subscript𝐶3C_{3}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 3 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, for otherwise, v𝑣vitalic_v and its two neighbors in H𝐻Hitalic_H together with w𝑤witalic_w would induce the claw K1,3subscript𝐾13K_{1,3}italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 , 3 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, a contradiction. Thus, since n⁢(H)≥3𝑛𝐻3n(H)\geq 3italic_n ( italic_H ) ≥ 3, at least one pair of vertices in HGsubscript𝐻𝐺H_{G}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, say v1subscript𝑣1v_{1}italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and vℓsubscript𝑣ℓv_{\ell}italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT (renaming if need be), which are dominated by distinct vertices in X𝑋Xitalic_X. Thus, av1subscript𝑎subscript𝑣1a_{v_{1}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is the unique vertex dominating vertices in the triangle-unit derived from v1subscript𝑣1v_{1}italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and avℓsubscript𝑎subscript𝑣ℓa_{v_{\ell}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is the unique vertex dominating the triangle-unit derived from vℓsubscript𝑣ℓv_{\ell}italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Next color blue the vertices in S=(X∖{avℓ})∪{bv1,cv1}𝑆𝑋subscript𝑎subscript𝑣ℓsubscript𝑏subscript𝑣1subscript𝑐subscript𝑣1S=(X\setminus\{a_{v_{\ell}}\})\cup\{b_{v_{1}},c_{v_{1}}\}italic_S = ( italic_X ∖ { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } ) ∪ { italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } and color white the vertices in V⁢(G)∖S𝑉𝐺𝑆V(G)\setminus Sitalic_V ( italic_G ) ∖ italic_S.

Under this coloring of vertices in V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ), observe that the only white-colored neighbor of bv1subscript𝑏subscript𝑣1b_{v_{1}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is cvℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑣ℓc_{v_{\ell}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and the only white colored neighbor of cv1subscript𝑐subscript𝑣1c_{v_{1}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is bv2subscript𝑏subscript𝑣2b_{v_{2}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Thus, the zero forcing process may begin by having bv1subscript𝑏subscript𝑣1b_{v_{1}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT force cvℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑣ℓc_{v_{\ell}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become colored blue, and also having cv1subscript𝑐subscript𝑣1c_{v_{1}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT force bv2subscript𝑏subscript𝑣2b_{v_{2}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become colored blue. Thereafter, the only white-colored neighbor of cv1subscript𝑐subscript𝑣1c_{v_{1}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would be bv2subscript𝑏subscript𝑣2b_{v_{2}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and so, cv1subscript𝑐subscript𝑣1c_{v_{1}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT may then force bv2subscript𝑏subscript𝑣2b_{v_{2}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become colored blue, and this process continues until bvℓsubscript𝑏subscript𝑣ℓb_{v_{\ell}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT becomes colored blue. Then after bvℓsubscript𝑏subscript𝑣ℓb_{v_{\ell}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT becomes colored blue, recall that bv1subscript𝑏subscript𝑣1b_{v_{1}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT had previously forced cvℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑣ℓc_{v_{\ell}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become colored blue. Thus, the only currently white-colored neighbor of bvℓsubscript𝑏subscript𝑣ℓb_{v_{\ell}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is avℓsubscript𝑎subscript𝑣ℓa_{v_{\ell}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and so, bvℓsubscript𝑏subscript𝑣ℓb_{v_{\ell}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would now force avℓsubscript𝑎subscript𝑣ℓa_{v_{\ell}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become colored blue; see Figure 3 for an illustration. Thus, all vertices in the cycle HGsubscript𝐻𝐺H_{G}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT become colored blue, and more so, the derived triangle-units from the vertices of HMsubscript𝐻𝑀H_{M}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_M end_POSTSUBSCRIPT contain only blue-colored vertices.

Let S⊆V⁢(G)𝑆𝑉𝐺S\subseteq V(G)italic_S ⊆ italic_V ( italic_G ) be the set of vertices obtained by either of the above initialization techniques. and thereafter, allow the zero forcing process to start and continue until no further color changes are possible. By the construction of the initial set of blue colored vertices in S𝑆Sitalic_S, observe the following properties:

  • •

    |S|=|X|+1𝑆𝑋1|S|=|X|+1| italic_S | = | italic_X | + 1.

  • •

    All vertices in the dominating set X𝑋Xitalic_X of G𝐺Gitalic_G are currently blue-colored.

  • •

    Each of triangle-units derived from the subgraph HMsubscript𝐻𝑀H_{M}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_M end_POSTSUBSCRIPT contain blue-colored vertices in G𝐺Gitalic_G.

  • •

    X𝑋Xitalic_X is reachable from S𝑆Sitalic_S.

If all vertices of G𝐺Gitalic_G have become blue-colored, then S𝑆Sitalic_S is a zero forcing set of G𝐺Gitalic_G, and so,

Z⁢(G)≤|S|=|X|+1=γ⁢(G)+1.𝑍𝐺𝑆𝑋1𝛾𝐺1Z(G)\leq|S|=|X|+1=\gamma(G)+1.italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ | italic_S | = | italic_X | + 1 = italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 1 .

Hence, we may assume that not all vertices of G𝐺Gitalic_G are reachable by S𝑆Sitalic_S since the desired result follows otherwise.

Suppose the zero forcing process stopped before all vertices in V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) become blue-colored. By constructing S𝑆Sitalic_S, we know that all vertices in PGsubscript𝑃𝐺P_{G}italic_P start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT are currently blue-colored, and further, that any derived triangle-unit from a vertex in HMsubscript𝐻𝑀H_{M}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_M end_POSTSUBSCRIPT will only contain blue-colored vertices in G𝐺Gitalic_G. We will now track color changes in G𝐺Gitalic_G through associated color changes in the triangle-units of G𝐺Gitalic_G, which we now define in terms of the multigraph MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. For a given blue and white coloring of V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) and a vertex v∈V⁢(MG)𝑣𝑉subscript𝑀𝐺v\in V(M_{G})italic_v ∈ italic_V ( italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ), we say that v𝑣vitalic_v (and also Tvsubscript𝑇𝑣T_{v}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT) is k3𝑘3\frac{k}{3}divide start_ARG italic_k end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored whenever V⁢(Tv)𝑉subscript𝑇𝑣V(T_{v})italic_V ( italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) contains exactly k𝑘kitalic_k blue currently colored vertices in G𝐺Gitalic_G, where k∈[3]𝑘delimited-[]3k\in[3]italic_k ∈ [ 3 ]; see Figure 4. Thus, there is a duality between blue and white colorings of V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) and k3𝑘3\frac{k}{3}divide start_ARG italic_k end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue colorings of the triangle-units in G𝐺Gitalic_G. That is, a coloring of V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) implies a coloring of the triangle-units of G𝐺Gitalic_G and vice versa. We define a blue-white-open edge to be an edge of G𝐺Gitalic_G with a blue-colored endpoint in a 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored triangle-unit and a white-colored endpoint in an adjacent triangle-unit.

The following two claims significantly simplify the monitoring of color changes in G𝐺Gitalic_G regarding the derived triangle units from vertices in MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT.

Claim 1.

Let v𝑣vitalic_v and w𝑤witalic_w be two adjacent vertices of MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT adjacent by at least one blue-white-open edge in G𝐺Gitalic_G. If v𝑣vitalic_v is 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored and w𝑤witalic_w is 1313\frac{1}{3}divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored, then w𝑤witalic_w becomes 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored by zero forcing color changes in G𝐺Gitalic_G.

Proof.

Let v𝑣vitalic_v and w𝑤witalic_w be two adjacent vertices of MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT which are adjacent by at least one blue-white-open edge in G𝐺Gitalic_G. Recalling our choice of notation, let Tv={av,bv,cv}subscript𝑇𝑣subscript𝑎𝑣subscript𝑏𝑣subscript𝑐𝑣T_{v}=\{a_{v},b_{v},c_{v}\}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } and V⁢(Tw)={aw,bw,cw}𝑉subscript𝑇𝑤subscript𝑎𝑤subscript𝑏𝑤subscript𝑐𝑤V(T_{w})=\{a_{w},b_{w},c_{w}\}italic_V ( italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) = { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } denote the vertices of the derived triangle-units from v𝑣vitalic_v, and w𝑤witalic_w, respectively. Suppose v𝑣vitalic_v is 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue colored and w𝑤witalic_w is 1313\frac{1}{3}divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue colored, where av⁢awsubscript𝑎𝑣subscript𝑎𝑤a_{v}a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is a blue-white-open edge connecting v𝑣vitalic_v to w𝑤witalic_w. Since w𝑤witalic_w is 1313\frac{1}{3}divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue colored, this supposition implies exactly one of the vertices in {bw,cw}subscript𝑏𝑤subscript𝑐𝑤\{b_{w},c_{w}\}{ italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } is currently blue-colored, say cwsubscript𝑐𝑤c_{w}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Moreover, since v𝑣vitalic_v is 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored, awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is the only white-colored neighbor of the blue-colored vertex avsubscript𝑎𝑣a_{v}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT in G𝐺Gitalic_G. Thus, avsubscript𝑎𝑣a_{v}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT may force awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become blue-colored. After awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT becomes blue-colored, the only white-colored neighbor of awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would be bwsubscript𝑏𝑤b_{w}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and so, awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT may then force bwsubscript𝑏𝑤b_{w}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become blue-colored. Therefore, by these aforementioned color changes in G𝐺Gitalic_G, the vertex w𝑤witalic_w in MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT becomes 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue colored. (□□\Box□)

Claim 2.

Let v𝑣vitalic_v and w𝑤witalic_w be two adjacent vertices of MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. If v𝑣vitalic_v is 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored and w𝑤witalic_w is 2323\frac{2}{3}divide start_ARG 2 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored, then w𝑤witalic_w will become 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored by way of zero forcing color changes in G𝐺Gitalic_G.

Proof.

Let v𝑣vitalic_v and w𝑤witalic_w be two adjacent vertices of MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and suppose v𝑣vitalic_v is 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored and w𝑤witalic_w is 2323\frac{2}{3}divide start_ARG 2 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored. Recalling our choice of notation, let Tv={av,bv,cv}subscript𝑇𝑣subscript𝑎𝑣subscript𝑏𝑣subscript𝑐𝑣T_{v}=\{a_{v},b_{v},c_{v}\}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } and V⁢(Tw)={aw,bw,cw}𝑉subscript𝑇𝑤subscript𝑎𝑤subscript𝑏𝑤subscript𝑐𝑤V(T_{w})=\{a_{w},b_{w},c_{w}\}italic_V ( italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) = { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } denote the vertices of the derived triangle-units from v𝑣vitalic_v, and w𝑤witalic_w, respectively. Let av⁢awsubscript𝑎𝑣subscript𝑎𝑤a_{v}a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be an edge of G𝐺Gitalic_G connecting the triangle-units Tvsubscript𝑇𝑣T_{v}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and Twsubscript𝑇𝑤T_{w}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Since v𝑣vitalic_v is 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored, every vertex in V⁢(Tv)𝑉subscript𝑇𝑣V(T_{v})italic_V ( italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) is blue-colored in G𝐺Gitalic_G. Since w𝑤witalic_w is 2323\frac{2}{3}divide start_ARG 2 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored, exactly two of the vertices in V⁢(Tw)𝑉subscript𝑇𝑤V(T_{w})italic_V ( italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) are blue-colored in G𝐺Gitalic_G. If awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is white-colored, then bwsubscript𝑏𝑤b_{w}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and cwsubscript𝑐𝑤c_{w}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT are blue-colored in G𝐺Gitalic_G. With this supposition, awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would then be the only white-colored neighbor of avsubscript𝑎𝑣a_{v}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and so, avsubscript𝑎𝑣a_{v}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would then force awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become blue-colored, resulting in w𝑤witalic_w becoming 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored. If awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT were currently blue-colored, then exactly one of the vertices in {bw,cw}subscript𝑏𝑤subscript𝑐𝑤\{b_{w},c_{w}\}{ italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } is blue-colored, say cwsubscript𝑐𝑤c_{w}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Thus, since avsubscript𝑎𝑣a_{v}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is blue-colored, the only white-colored neighbor of awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would be bwsubscript𝑏𝑤b_{w}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and so, awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would then force bwsubscript𝑏𝑤b_{w}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become blue-colored. Hence, w𝑤witalic_w becomes 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored. (□□\Box□)

With the above claims proven, we now return to the current state of blue and white colored vertices in V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ). Since each vertex in HMsubscript𝐻𝑀H_{M}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_M end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored, we know the current set of 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored vertices is nonempty. Let B𝐵Bitalic_B be the current set of 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored vertices in MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Since MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is a connected multigraph, and not all vertices of G𝐺Gitalic_G are blue-colored, at least one vertex of MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT must exist that is (noninclusive) adjacent to a vertex in B𝐵Bitalic_B. Let w∈V⁢(MG)𝑤𝑉subscript𝑀𝐺w\in V(M_{G})italic_w ∈ italic_V ( italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) be a vertex adjacent to the set B𝐵Bitalic_B in MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Note that w𝑤witalic_w cannot be 0303\frac{0}{3}divide start_ARG 0 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored, since if so, one of the vertices in B𝐵Bitalic_B would have forced onto Twsubscript𝑇𝑤T_{w}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT making w𝑤witalic_w at least 1313\frac{1}{3}divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored. If w𝑤witalic_w were 2323\frac{2}{3}divide start_ARG 2 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored, then by Claim 2, w𝑤witalic_w would become 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored, and hence belong to B𝐵Bitalic_B. Thus, w𝑤witalic_w must be 1313\frac{1}{3}divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored. However, by Claim 1, if w𝑤witalic_w were adjacent with B𝐵Bitalic_B by at least one blue-white-open edge in G𝐺Gitalic_G, then w𝑤witalic_w would become 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored, and hence belong to B𝐵Bitalic_B. Hence, w𝑤witalic_w is 1313\frac{1}{3}divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored, and no edge connecting w𝑤witalic_w to B𝐵Bitalic_B is blue-white-open. Both endpoints of exactly one edge connecting w𝑤witalic_w to B𝐵Bitalic_B are blue-colored.

Since we are assuming that S𝑆Sitalic_S is not a zero forcing set G𝐺Gitalic_G, we now define a collection of extension rules that modify the set S𝑆Sitalic_S so that after each application of the said rule, more vertices become colored blue; also we maintain the cardinality of our original set S𝑆Sitalic_S. For each rule we introduce, we will formally state it and then prove a claim for its validity. For example, see the first simple rule given below.

Extension Rule I.

Let w∉B𝑤𝐵w\notin Bitalic_w ∉ italic_B be adjacent with v∈B𝑣𝐵v\in Bitalic_v ∈ italic_B in MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. If av⁢awsubscript𝑎𝑣subscript𝑎𝑤a_{v}a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is the edge connecting v𝑣vitalic_v to w𝑤witalic_w in G𝐺Gitalic_G and avsubscript𝑎𝑣a_{v}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT did not force during the zero forcing process previously, then

S←(S∖{aw})∪{bw}.←𝑆𝑆subscript𝑎𝑤subscript𝑏𝑤S\leftarrow(S\setminus\{a_{w}\})\cup\{b_{w}\}.italic_S ← ( italic_S ∖ { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } ) ∪ { italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } .
Claim 3.

Extension Rule I is a valid-z𝑧zitalic_z-proper-extension rule on S𝑆Sitalic_S.

Proof.

Let w∉B𝑤𝐵w\notin Bitalic_w ∉ italic_B is adjacent with v∈B𝑣𝐵v\in Bitalic_v ∈ italic_B in MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Recalling our choice of notation, let Tv={av,bv,cv}subscript𝑇𝑣subscript𝑎𝑣subscript𝑏𝑣subscript𝑐𝑣T_{v}=\{a_{v},b_{v},c_{v}\}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } and V⁢(Tw)={aw,bw,cw}𝑉subscript𝑇𝑤subscript𝑎𝑤subscript𝑏𝑤subscript𝑐𝑤V(T_{w})=\{a_{w},b_{w},c_{w}\}italic_V ( italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) = { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } denote the vertices of the derived triangle-units from v𝑣vitalic_v, and w𝑤witalic_w, respectively. Next, suppose av⁢awsubscript𝑎𝑣subscript𝑎𝑤a_{v}a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is the edge connecting v𝑣vitalic_v to w𝑤witalic_w in G𝐺Gitalic_G, and, further, that avsubscript𝑎𝑣a_{v}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT did not force during the zero forcing process previously. Note awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is blue-colored. Let S′=(S∖{aw})∪{bw}superscript𝑆′𝑆subscript𝑎𝑤subscript𝑏𝑤S^{\prime}=(S\setminus\{a_{w}\})\cup\{b_{w}\}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = ( italic_S ∖ { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } ) ∪ { italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT }, and so, |S′|=|S|superscript𝑆′𝑆|S^{\prime}|=|S|| italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | = | italic_S |. Thus, it remains to show NGz⁢[S]⊂NGz⁢[S′]superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]𝑆superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]superscript𝑆′N_{G}^{z}[S]\subset N_{G}^{z}[S^{\prime}]italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S ] ⊂ italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ]. Since avsubscript𝑎𝑣a_{v}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT did not force a color change during the zero forcing process, and since bwsubscript𝑏𝑤b_{w}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and cwsubscript𝑐𝑤c_{w}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT are white-colored, removing awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT does not affect any color changes that occurred by applying the zero forcing process on S𝑆Sitalic_S in G𝐺Gitalic_G. Thus, all color changes due to S𝑆Sitalic_S will also occur with S′superscript𝑆′S^{\prime}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. In particular, at some point in the zero forcing process, each vertex in the triangle-unit Tvsubscript𝑇𝑣T_{v}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is colored blue, after which awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would be the only white-colored neighbor of avsubscript𝑎𝑣a_{v}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Therefore, avsubscript𝑎𝑣a_{v}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT may then force awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become colored blue. Thus, NGz⁢[S]⊆NGz⁢[S′]superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]𝑆superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]superscript𝑆′N_{G}^{z}[S]\subseteq N_{G}^{z}[S^{\prime}]italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S ] ⊆ italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ]. However, since bw∉NGz⁢[S]subscript𝑏𝑤superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]𝑆b_{w}\notin N_{G}^{z}[S]italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ∉ italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S ] and bw∈NGz⁢[S′]subscript𝑏𝑤superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]superscript𝑆′b_{w}\in N_{G}^{z}[S^{\prime}]italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ∈ italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ], we establish NGz⁢[S]⊂NGz⁢[S′]superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]𝑆superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]superscript𝑆′N_{G}^{z}[S]\subset N_{G}^{z}[S^{\prime}]italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S ] ⊂ italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ], and the desired result follows. (□□\Box□)

By Claim 3, we may apply Extension Rule I as many times as needed, always producing a new initial set of S𝑆Sitalic_S of blue-colored vertices in G𝐺Gitalic_G and always maintaining |S|=|X|+1𝑆𝑋1|S|=|X|+1| italic_S | = | italic_X | + 1. If we eventually arrive at a set S𝑆Sitalic_S of blue-colored vertices so that all of V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) becomes blue-colored, then S𝑆Sitalic_S is a zero forcing set of G𝐺Gitalic_G. Thus,

Z⁢(G)≤|S|=|X|+1=γ⁢(G)+1.𝑍𝐺𝑆𝑋1𝛾𝐺1Z(G)\leq|S|=|X|+1=\gamma(G)+1.italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ | italic_S | = | italic_X | + 1 = italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 1 .

Therefore, we will assume that not all of V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) has become blue-colored since otherwise, the desired result will follow.

Let z𝑧zitalic_z be a 1313\frac{1}{3}divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue colored vertex of MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT adjacent with the set B𝐵Bitalic_B and note that Extension Rule I implies the blue-colored vertex of Tzsubscript𝑇𝑧T_{z}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT was not originally colored in the set S𝑆Sitalic_S. Thus, the blue-colored vertex in Tzsubscript𝑇𝑧T_{z}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT must have originally been white-colored and become blue-colored by the zero forcing process in G𝐺Gitalic_G. Let azsubscript𝑎𝑧a_{z}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be the vertex of Tzsubscript𝑇𝑧T_{z}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT that became blue-colored during the zero forcing process in G𝐺Gitalic_G, and so, azsubscript𝑎𝑧a_{z}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is the endpoint of some edge whose other endpoint is in B𝐵Bitalic_B. There are two possible cases for the vertices in the triangle-unit Tzsubscript𝑇𝑧T_{z}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT concerning the dominating set X𝑋Xitalic_X of G𝐺Gitalic_G; either V⁢(Tz)∩X≠∅𝑉subscript𝑇𝑧𝑋V(T_{z})\cap X\neq\emptysetitalic_V ( italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) ∩ italic_X ≠ ∅ or V⁢(Tz)∩X=∅𝑉subscript𝑇𝑧𝑋V(T_{z})\cap X=\emptysetitalic_V ( italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) ∩ italic_X = ∅; see Figure 5. If az∈Xsubscript𝑎𝑧𝑋a_{z}\in Xitalic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ∈ italic_X, we are in the configuration shown by Figure 5 (a). In this case, recall that all vertices of X𝑋Xitalic_X are currently colored blue (either in S𝑆Sitalic_S or by some forcing step). Thus, bzsubscript𝑏𝑧b_{z}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and czsubscript𝑐𝑧c_{z}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT belong to some component of G⁢[Y]𝐺delimited-[]𝑌G[Y]italic_G [ italic_Y ], say HGzsuperscriptsubscript𝐻𝐺𝑧H_{G}^{z}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, and this component is either a path or a cycle. We are considering both possibilities now.

Suppose first that av∈Xsubscript𝑎𝑣𝑋a_{v}\in Xitalic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ∈ italic_X forces a neighbor azsubscript𝑎𝑧a_{z}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become blue-colored. Since bzsubscript𝑏𝑧b_{z}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and czsubscript𝑐𝑧c_{z}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT must be dominated by a vertex in X𝑋Xitalic_X, bzsubscript𝑏𝑧b_{z}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and czsubscript𝑐𝑧c_{z}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT have a neighbor in X𝑋Xitalic_X, say axsubscript𝑎𝑥a_{x}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_x end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, respectively. Since all vertices of X𝑋Xitalic_X are reachable from S𝑆Sitalic_S, we remark that axsubscript𝑎𝑥a_{x}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_x end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT are currently blue-colored.

We next introduce an Extension Rule for handling this case, provided Extension Rule I has been applied as many times as possible prior.

Extension Rule II.

Let z∉B𝑧𝐵z\notin Bitalic_z ∉ italic_B be adjacent with v∈B𝑣𝐵v\in Bitalic_v ∈ italic_B in MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. If av∈Xsubscript𝑎𝑣𝑋a_{v}\in Xitalic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ∈ italic_X forces az∉Xsubscript𝑎𝑧𝑋a_{z}\notin Xitalic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ∉ italic_X to become blue-colored, then

S←(S∖{aw})∪{bz}.←𝑆𝑆subscript𝑎𝑤subscript𝑏𝑧S\leftarrow(S\setminus\{a_{w}\})\cup\{b_{z}\}.italic_S ← ( italic_S ∖ { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } ) ∪ { italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } .
Claim 4.

Extension Rule II is a valid-z𝑧zitalic_z-proper-extension rule on S𝑆Sitalic_S

Proof.

Note that since the ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition of V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) contains no diamond-unit, ax≠awsubscript𝑎𝑥subscript𝑎𝑤a_{x}\neq a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_x end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ≠ italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Let S′=(S∖{aw})∪{bz}superscript𝑆′𝑆subscript𝑎𝑤subscript𝑏𝑧S^{\prime}=(S\setminus\{a_{w}\})\cup\{b_{z}\}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = ( italic_S ∖ { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } ) ∪ { italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT }, and so, |S′|=|S|superscript𝑆′𝑆|S^{\prime}|=|S|| italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | = | italic_S |. Thus, it remains to show NGz⁢[S]⊂NGz⁢[S′]superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]𝑆superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]superscript𝑆′N_{G}^{z}[S]\subset N_{G}^{z}[S^{\prime}]italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S ] ⊂ italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ]. If any neighbor of awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT forced during the zero forcing process under the coloring starting with S𝑆Sitalic_S, then all vertices of the triangle-unit Tysubscript𝑇𝑦T_{y}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_y end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would necessarily be colored blue, which implies that y𝑦yitalic_y was 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored. Thus, y𝑦yitalic_y may force onto Tzsubscript𝑇𝑧T_{z}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT across the blue-white-open edge aw⁢czsubscript𝑎𝑤subscript𝑐𝑧a_{w}c_{z}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. By Claim 1, Tzsubscript𝑇𝑧T_{z}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would then become 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored, a contradiction since bzsubscript𝑏𝑧b_{z}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and czsubscript𝑐𝑧c_{z}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT are currently white-colored. Thus, no neighbor of awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT forces a color change during the zero forcing process starting with S𝑆Sitalic_S. Therefore, all color changes previously due to S𝑆Sitalic_S will also occur by starting the zero forcing process with S′superscript𝑆′S^{\prime}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. In particular, at some point of the zero forcing process, avsubscript𝑎𝑣a_{v}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT will force azsubscript𝑎𝑧a_{z}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become blue-colored. Thereafter, the only white-colored neighbor of azsubscript𝑎𝑧a_{z}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would be czsubscript𝑐𝑧c_{z}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, so azsubscript𝑎𝑧a_{z}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT may force czsubscript𝑐𝑧c_{z}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become blue-colored. After czsubscript𝑐𝑧c_{z}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT becomes blue-colored, the only white-colored neighbor of czsubscript𝑐𝑧c_{z}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would be awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and so, cwsubscript𝑐𝑤c_{w}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT may force awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become blue-colored. Thus, NGz⁢[S′]=NGz⁢[S]∪{bz,cz}superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]superscript𝑆′superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]𝑆subscript𝑏𝑧subscript𝑐𝑧N_{G}^{z}[S^{\prime}]=N_{G}^{z}[S]\cup\{b_{z},c_{z}\}italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ] = italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S ] ∪ { italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT }. Hence, NGz⁢[S]⊂NGz⁢[S′]superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]𝑆superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]superscript𝑆′N_{G}^{z}[S]\subset N_{G}^{z}[S^{\prime}]italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S ] ⊂ italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ], and the proof of the claim is finished. (□□\Box□)

Apply Extension Rule II, and note that (as seen in the proof of Claim 4), that x𝑥xitalic_x may become 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored by applying Extension Rule I, while we do not yet have a rule for w𝑤witalic_w. Continue the zero forcing process and apply Extension Rule I whenever possible until no further color changes are possible. If w𝑤witalic_w does not become 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored, then we are in the case as seen by Figure 5 (b), and we proceed to the following two extension rules given below; otherwise y𝑦yitalic_y becomes 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored and we may once again apply Extension Rule II. That is, you are not allowed to apply Extension Rule II again unless you have first ensured that all triangle-units containing a “swapped dominating vertex” used by an extension rule are 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored. Since awsubscript𝑎𝑤a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is a dominating vertex, we note that bwsubscript𝑏𝑤b_{w}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and cwsubscript𝑐𝑤c_{w}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT must belong to some component of G⁢[Y]𝐺delimited-[]𝑌G[Y]italic_G [ italic_Y ], say HGwsuperscriptsubscript𝐻𝐺𝑤H_{G}^{w}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT.

Suppose that HGwsuperscriptsubscript𝐻𝐺𝑤H_{G}^{w}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is a path component of G⁢[Y]𝐺delimited-[]𝑌G[Y]italic_G [ italic_Y ]. Denote to the vertices of this component by HGw:bw1⁢cw1⁢…⁢bwℓ⁢cwℓ:superscriptsubscript𝐻𝐺𝑤subscript𝑏subscript𝑤1subscript𝑐subscript𝑤1…subscript𝑏subscript𝑤ℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑤ℓH_{G}^{w}:b_{w_{1}}c_{w_{1}}\dots b_{w_{\ell}}c_{w_{\ell}}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT : italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT … italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and denote the associated vertices (triangle-units) in MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT by HMw:w1⁢w2⁢…⁢wℓ:superscriptsubscript𝐻𝑀𝑤subscript𝑤1subscript𝑤2…subscript𝑤ℓH_{M}^{w}:w_{1}w_{2}\dots w_{\ell}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_M end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT : italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT … italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Note for some i∈[ℓ]𝑖delimited-[]ℓi\in[\ell]italic_i ∈ [ roman_ℓ ], awi=awsubscript𝑎subscript𝑤𝑖subscript𝑎𝑤a_{w_{i}}=a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, bwi=bwsubscript𝑏subscript𝑤𝑖subscript𝑏𝑤b_{w_{i}}=b_{w}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and cwi=cwsubscript𝑐subscript𝑤𝑖subscript𝑐𝑤c_{w_{i}}=c_{w}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Since HGwsuperscriptsubscript𝐻𝐺𝑤H_{G}^{w}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is a path component of G⁢[Y]𝐺delimited-[]𝑌G[Y]italic_G [ italic_Y ] and since G𝐺Gitalic_G is cubic, the enpoints of HGwsuperscriptsubscript𝐻𝐺𝑤H_{G}^{w}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT are necessarily adjacent with two vertices from the dominating set X𝑋Xitalic_X of G𝐺Gitalic_G, say ay′subscript𝑎superscript𝑦′a_{y^{\prime}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_y start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and aw1subscript𝑎subscript𝑤1a_{w_{1}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT for bw1subscript𝑏subscript𝑤1b_{w_{1}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and awℓsubscript𝑎subscript𝑤ℓa_{w_{\ell}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and ay′′subscript𝑎superscript𝑦′′a_{y^{\prime\prime}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_y start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT for cwℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑤ℓc_{w_{\ell}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT; see Figure 7 for an illustration of this configuration. The following extension rule addresses this possibility.

Extension Rule III.

Let avsubscript𝑎𝑣a_{v}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be a vertex that forced the dominating vertex awisubscript𝑎subscript𝑤𝑖a_{w_{i}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT in the triangle-unit Twisubscript𝑇subscript𝑤𝑖T_{w_{i}}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT with vertex set V⁢(Twi)={awi,bwi,cwi}𝑉subscript𝑇subscript𝑤𝑖subscript𝑎subscript𝑤𝑖subscript𝑏subscript𝑤𝑖subscript𝑐subscript𝑤𝑖V(T_{w_{i}})=\{a_{w_{i}},b_{w_{i}},c_{w_{i}}\}italic_V ( italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) = { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT }. If the component HGsubscript𝐻𝐺H_{G}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT containing bwisubscript𝑏subscript𝑤𝑖b_{w_{i}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and cwisubscript𝑐subscript𝑤𝑖c_{w_{i}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is a path, then

S←(S∖{ay′′})∪{bwi},←𝑆𝑆subscript𝑎superscript𝑦′′subscript𝑏subscript𝑤𝑖S\leftarrow(S\setminus\{a_{y^{\prime\prime}}\})\cup\{b_{w_{i}}\},italic_S ← ( italic_S ∖ { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_y start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } ) ∪ { italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } ,

where ay′′subscript𝑎superscript𝑦′′a_{y^{\prime\prime}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_y start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is adjacent with cwℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑤ℓc_{w_{\ell}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT (and ay′′∉Twisubscript𝑎superscript𝑦′′subscript𝑇subscript𝑤𝑖a_{y^{\prime\prime}}\notin T_{w_{i}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_y start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ∉ italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT).

With the following claim, we establish the validity of Extension Rule III.

Claim 5.

Extension Rule III is a valid-z𝑧zitalic_z-proper-extension rule on S𝑆Sitalic_S.

Proof.

Let S′=(S∖{ay′′})∪{bwi}superscript𝑆′𝑆subscript𝑎superscript𝑦′′subscript𝑏subscript𝑤𝑖S^{\prime}=(S\setminus\{a_{y^{\prime\prime}}\})\cup\{b_{w_{i}}\}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = ( italic_S ∖ { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_y start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } ) ∪ { italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT }, and so, |S′|=|S|superscript𝑆′𝑆|S^{\prime}|=|S|| italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | = | italic_S |. Thus is remains to show that NGz⁢[S]⊂NGz⁢[S′]superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]𝑆superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]superscript𝑆′N_{G}^{z}[S]\subset N_{G}^{z}[S^{\prime}]italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S ] ⊂ italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ]. If any neighbor of ay′′subscript𝑎superscript𝑦′′a_{y^{\prime\prime}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_y start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT forced during the zero forcing process under the coloring starting with S𝑆Sitalic_S, then all vertices of the triangle-unit Ty′′subscript𝑇superscript𝑦′′T_{y^{\prime\prime}}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_y start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would necessarily be blue-colored, which implies that y′′superscript𝑦′′y^{\prime\prime}italic_y start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT was 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored. Thus, y′′superscript𝑦′′y^{\prime\prime}italic_y start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT may then forced onto Twℓsubscript𝑇subscript𝑤ℓT_{w_{\ell}}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT across the blue-white-open edge ay′′⁢bwℓsubscript𝑎superscript𝑦′′subscript𝑏subscript𝑤ℓa_{y^{\prime\prime}}b_{w_{\ell}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_y start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. By Claim 1, Twℓsubscript𝑇subscript𝑤ℓT_{w_{\ell}}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would then become 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored. There after, Twℓsubscript𝑇subscript𝑤ℓT_{w_{\ell}}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT could then force onto Twℓ−1subscript𝑇subscript𝑤ℓ1T_{w_{\ell-1}}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT by the blue-white-open edge bwℓ⁢cwℓ−1subscript𝑏subscript𝑤ℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑤ℓ1b_{w_{\ell}}c_{w_{\ell-1}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, which in turn would make wℓ−1subscript𝑤ℓ1w_{\ell-1}italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT a 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored vertex.
