This process would continue until wisubscript𝑤𝑖w_{i}italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is forced onto, making it a 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored vertex, a contradiction since bwisubscript𝑏subscript𝑤𝑖b_{w_{i}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and cwisubscript𝑐subscript𝑤𝑖c_{w_{i}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT are currently white-colored. Thus, no neighbor of ay′′subscript𝑎superscript𝑦′′a_{y^{\prime\prime}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_y start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT forces a color change during the zero forcing process starting with S𝑆Sitalic_S. Therefore, all color changes previously due to S𝑆Sitalic_S will also occur by starting the zero forcing process at S′superscript𝑆′S^{\prime}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT.

Let the zero forcing process begin from S′superscript𝑆′S^{\prime}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT until awisubscript𝑎subscript𝑤𝑖a_{w_{i}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, after which, cwisubscript𝑐subscript𝑤𝑖c_{w_{i}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would then be the only white-colored neighbor of awisubscript𝑎subscript𝑤𝑖a_{w_{i}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Thus, awisubscript𝑎subscript𝑤𝑖a_{w_{i}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT may then force cwisubscript𝑐subscript𝑤𝑖c_{w_{i}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become blue colored. After cwisubscript𝑐subscript𝑤𝑖c_{w_{i}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT becomes blue-colored, wisubscript𝑤𝑖w_{i}italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would be a 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored vertex of MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Moreover, wisubscript𝑤𝑖w_{i}italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is connected to the 1313\frac{1}{3}divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored neighbors wi−1subscript𝑤𝑖1w_{i-1}italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and wi+1subscript𝑤𝑖1w_{i+1}italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i + 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT by blue-white-open edges cwi−1⁢bwisubscript𝑐subscript𝑤𝑖1subscript𝑏subscript𝑤𝑖c_{w_{i-1}}b_{w_{i}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and cwi⁢bwi+1subscript𝑐subscript𝑤𝑖subscript𝑏subscript𝑤𝑖1c_{w_{i}}b_{w_{i+1}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i + 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, respectively. By Claim 1, wi−1subscript𝑤𝑖1w_{i-1}italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and wi+1subscript𝑤𝑖1w_{i+1}italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i + 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would then become 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored. Thereafter, this process would continue until both w1subscript𝑤1w_{1}italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and wℓsubscript𝑤ℓw_{\ell}italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT become 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored. After wℓsubscript𝑤ℓw_{\ell}italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT becomes 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored, the only white colored neighbor of cwℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑤ℓc_{w_{\ell}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would be ay′′subscript𝑎superscript𝑦′′a_{y^{\prime\prime}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_y start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and so, cwℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑤ℓc_{w_{\ell}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT may then force ay′′subscript𝑎superscript𝑦′′a_{y^{\prime\prime}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_y start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become blue-colored; see Figure 8 for an illustration. Note that all vertices in the dominating set X𝑋Xitalic_X of G𝐺Gitalic_G remain reachable from S′superscript𝑆′S^{\prime}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. Thus, NGz⁢[S]⊂NGz⁢[S′]superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]𝑆superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]superscript𝑆′N_{G}^{z}[S]\subset N_{G}^{z}[S^{\prime}]italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S ] ⊂ italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ], and the proof of the claim is finished. (□□\Box□)

If HGwsuperscriptsubscript𝐻𝐺𝑤H_{G}^{w}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT was not a path component of G⁢[Y]𝐺delimited-[]𝑌G[Y]italic_G [ italic_Y ], then HGwsuperscriptsubscript𝐻𝐺𝑤H_{G}^{w}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is necessarily a cycle component of G⁢[Y]𝐺delimited-[]𝑌G[Y]italic_G [ italic_Y ]. Denote to the vertices of this component by HGw:bw1⁢cw1⁢…⁢bwℓ⁢cwℓ⁢bw1:superscriptsubscript𝐻𝐺𝑤subscript𝑏subscript𝑤1subscript𝑐subscript𝑤1…subscript𝑏subscript𝑤ℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑤ℓsubscript𝑏subscript𝑤1H_{G}^{w}:b_{w_{1}}c_{w_{1}}\dots b_{w_{\ell}}c_{w_{\ell}}b_{w_{1}}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT : italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT … italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and denote the associated vertices (triangle-units) in MGsubscript𝑀𝐺M_{G}italic_M start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT by HMw:w1⁢w2⁢…⁢wℓ⁢w1:superscriptsubscript𝐻𝑀𝑤subscript𝑤1subscript𝑤2…subscript𝑤ℓsubscript𝑤1H_{M}^{w}:w_{1}w_{2}\dots w_{\ell}w_{1}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_M end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT : italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT … italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Note for some i∈[ℓ]𝑖delimited-[]ℓi\in[\ell]italic_i ∈ [ roman_ℓ ], awi=awsubscript𝑎subscript𝑤𝑖subscript𝑎𝑤a_{w_{i}}=a_{w}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, bwi=bwsubscript𝑏subscript𝑤𝑖subscript𝑏𝑤b_{w_{i}}=b_{w}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and cwi=cwsubscript𝑐subscript𝑤𝑖subscript𝑐𝑤c_{w_{i}}=c_{w}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. configuration.

We introduce the following extension rule to deal with cycle components of G⁢[Y]𝐺delimited-[]𝑌G[Y]italic_G [ italic_Y ].

Extension Rule IV.

Let avsubscript𝑎𝑣a_{v}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be a vertex that forced the dominating vertex awisubscript𝑎subscript𝑤𝑖a_{w_{i}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT in the triangle-unit Twisubscript𝑇subscript𝑤𝑖T_{w_{i}}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT with vertex set V⁢(Twi)={awi,bwi,cwi}𝑉subscript𝑇subscript𝑤𝑖subscript𝑎subscript𝑤𝑖subscript𝑏subscript𝑤𝑖subscript𝑐subscript𝑤𝑖V(T_{w_{i}})=\{a_{w_{i}},b_{w_{i}},c_{w_{i}}\}italic_V ( italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) = { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT }. If the component HGsubscript𝐻𝐺H_{G}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT containing bwisubscript𝑏subscript𝑤𝑖b_{w_{i}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and cwisubscript𝑐subscript𝑤𝑖c_{w_{i}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is a cycle, then

S←(S∖{awℓ})∪{bwi},←𝑆𝑆subscript𝑎subscript𝑤ℓsubscript𝑏subscript𝑤𝑖S\leftarrow(S\setminus\{a_{w_{\ell}}\})\cup\{b_{w_{i}}\},italic_S ← ( italic_S ∖ { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } ) ∪ { italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } ,

where ay′′subscript𝑎superscript𝑦′′a_{y^{\prime\prime}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_y start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is adjacent with cwℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑤ℓc_{w_{\ell}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT (and ay′′∉Twisubscript𝑎superscript𝑦′′subscript𝑇subscript𝑤𝑖a_{y^{\prime\prime}}\notin T_{w_{i}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_y start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ∉ italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT).

The following claim verifies the validity of Extension Rule IV.

Claim 6.

Extension Rule IV is a valid-z𝑧zitalic_z-proper-extension rule on S𝑆Sitalic_S.

Proof.

Let S′=(S∖{awℓ})∪{bwi}superscript𝑆′𝑆subscript𝑎subscript𝑤ℓsubscript𝑏subscript𝑤𝑖S^{\prime}=(S\setminus\{a_{w_{\ell}}\})\cup\{b_{w_{i}}\}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = ( italic_S ∖ { italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } ) ∪ { italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT }, and so, |S′|=|S|superscript𝑆′𝑆|S^{\prime}|=|S|| italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | = | italic_S |. Note that we have only swapped one vertex from the dominating set X𝑋Xitalic_X of G𝐺Gitalic_G; see Extension Rule II. Thus, no vertex awjsubscript𝑎subscript𝑤𝑗a_{w_{j}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, for j∈[ℓ]∖{i}𝑗delimited-[]ℓ𝑖j\in[\ell]\setminus\{i\}italic_j ∈ [ roman_ℓ ] ∖ { italic_i }, has been forced to become blue-colored by the zero forcing process in G𝐺Gitalic_G; that is, each of these vertices is initially blue-colored in S𝑆Sitalic_S. If vertex adjacent with awℓsubscript𝑎subscript𝑤ℓa_{w_{\ell}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT was contained in a 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored triangle-unit, then we could have applied Extension Rule I and then forced wℓsubscript𝑤ℓw_{\ell}italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored. If awℓsubscript𝑎subscript𝑤ℓa_{w_{\ell}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT was not contained in a 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored triangle-unit, then necessarily no neighbor of awℓsubscript𝑎subscript𝑤ℓa_{w_{\ell}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT forced a color change during the zero forcing process starting with S𝑆Sitalic_S. Thus, all color changes under the set S𝑆Sitalic_S will also occur from starting with set S′superscript𝑆′S^{\prime}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT.

Starting from the set S′superscript𝑆′S^{\prime}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, let the zero forcing process start and continue. At some iteration of the zero forcing process, avsubscript𝑎𝑣a_{v}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_v end_POSTSUBSCRIPT will force awisubscript𝑎subscript𝑤𝑖a_{w_{i}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become blue-colored. Thereafter, cwisubscript𝑐subscript𝑤𝑖c_{w_{i}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would be the only white-colored neighbor of awisubscript𝑎subscript𝑤𝑖a_{w_{i}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Thus, awisubscript𝑎subscript𝑤𝑖a_{w_{i}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT may then force cwisubscript𝑐subscript𝑤𝑖c_{w_{i}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become blue-colored. After cwisubscript𝑐subscript𝑤𝑖c_{w_{i}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT becomes blue-colored, wisubscript𝑤𝑖w_{i}italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would be 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored. Thus, wisubscript𝑤𝑖w_{i}italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT may then force onto the triangle-units Twi−1subscript𝑇subscript𝑤𝑖1T_{w_{i-1}}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and Twi+1subscript𝑇subscript𝑤𝑖1T_{w_{i+1}}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i + 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT along the blue-white open edges bwi⁢cwi−1subscript𝑏subscript𝑤𝑖subscript𝑐subscript𝑤𝑖1b_{w_{i}}c_{w_{i-1}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and cwi⁢bwi+1subscript𝑐subscript𝑤𝑖subscript𝑏subscript𝑤𝑖1c_{w_{i}}b_{w_{i+1}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i + 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, respectively. In particular, a sequence of color changes occurs until w1subscript𝑤1w_{1}italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT becomes 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored. Thereafter, cwℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑤ℓc_{w_{\ell}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is the only white-colored neighbor of bw1subscript𝑏subscript𝑤1b_{w_{1}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and so, bw1subscript𝑏subscript𝑤1b_{w_{1}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT may then force cwℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑤ℓc_{w_{\ell}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become blue-colored. Note that wℓsubscript𝑤ℓw_{\ell}italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT was also forced onto by wℓ−1subscript𝑤ℓ1w_{\ell-1}italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and so, bwℓsubscript𝑏subscript𝑤ℓb_{w_{\ell}}italic_b start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is blue-colored. Thus, at some iteration of the zero forcing process starting with S′superscript𝑆′S^{\prime}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, awℓsubscript𝑎subscript𝑤ℓa_{w_{\ell}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would be the only white-colored neighbor of cwℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑤ℓc_{w_{\ell}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Hence, cwℓsubscript𝑐subscript𝑤ℓc_{w_{\ell}}italic_c start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT may then force awℓsubscript𝑎subscript𝑤ℓa_{w_{\ell}}italic_a start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_w start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ℓ end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become colored blue; see Figure 10 for an illustration. Therefore, NGz⁢[S]⊂NGz⁢[S′]superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]𝑆superscriptsubscript𝑁𝐺𝑧delimited-[]superscript𝑆′N_{G}^{z}[S]\subset N_{G}^{z}[S^{\prime}]italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S ] ⊂ italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_z end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT [ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ], and the proof of the claim is finished. (□□\Box□)

Apply Extension Rule III if HGwsuperscriptsubscript𝐻𝐺𝑤H_{G}^{w}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is a path component of G⁢[Y]𝐺delimited-[]𝑌G[Y]italic_G [ italic_Y ], and Extension Rule IV if HGwsuperscriptsubscript𝐻𝐺𝑤H_{G}^{w}italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_G end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_w end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is a cycle component of G⁢[Y]𝐺delimited-[]𝑌G[Y]italic_G [ italic_Y ]. Thereafter, allow the zero forcing process to continue and apply Extension Rule I whenever possibly until no further color changes are possible. If a triangle-unit that we swapped a dominating vertex does not become 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored, then apply either Extension Rule III or Extension Rule IV, depending on the component of G⁢[Y]𝐺delimited-[]𝑌G[Y]italic_G [ italic_Y ] in consideration. After all, borrowed from triangle-units become 3333\frac{3}{3}divide start_ARG 3 end_ARG start_ARG 3 end_ARG-blue-colored, apply Extension Rule II if need be and repeat. Since G𝐺Gitalic_G is connected, we eventually arrive at a set S𝑆Sitalic_S of blue-colored vertices so that all of V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) becomes blue-colored, and so, the resulting set S𝑆Sitalic_S is a zero forcing set of G𝐺Gitalic_G. Thus,

Z⁢(G)≤|S|=|X|+1=γ⁢(G)+1,𝑍𝐺𝑆𝑋1𝛾𝐺1Z(G)\leq|S|=|X|+1=\gamma(G)+1,italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ | italic_S | = | italic_X | + 1 = italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 1 ,

and the proof of the lemma is finished. ∎

In the next section, we give our proof for Conjecture 1.

3 Proof of Conjecture 1

In this section, we prove Conjecture 1 and characterize graphs attaining equality in its statement.

Theorem 8.

If G𝐺Gitalic_G is a connected, cubic, and claw-free graph, then

Z⁢(G)≤γ⁢(G)+2,𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺2Z(G)\leq\gamma(G)+2,italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2 ,

with equality if and only if G∈𝒩cubic∗𝐺superscriptsubscript𝒩cubicG\in\mathcal{N}_{\text{cubic}}^{*}italic_G ∈ caligraphic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT cubic end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT.

Proof.

We will proceed by induction on the order n≥4𝑛4n\geq 4italic_n ≥ 4 of a connected, cubic, and claw-free graph. If n=4𝑛4n=4italic_n = 4, then G=K4𝐺subscript𝐾4G=K_{4}italic_G = italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 4 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT implying Z⁢(G)=γ⁢(G)+2𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺2Z(G)=\gamma(G)+2italic_Z ( italic_G ) = italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2; see Figure 11 (a). If n=6𝑛6n=6italic_n = 6, then G𝐺Gitalic_G is the prism C3⁢□⁢K2subscript𝐶3□subscript𝐾2C_{3}\>\Box\>K_{2}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 3 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT □ italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT implying Z⁢(G)=3<γ⁢(G)+2=4𝑍𝐺3𝛾𝐺24Z(G)=3<\gamma(G)+2=4italic_Z ( italic_G ) = 3 < italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2 = 4; see Figure 11 (b). If n=8𝑛8n=8italic_n = 8, then G𝐺Gitalic_G is the diamond-necklace N2subscript𝑁2N_{2}italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT which implies Z⁢(G)=γ⁢(G)+2𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺2Z(G)=\gamma(G)+2italic_Z ( italic_G ) = italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2 by Lemma 6; also see Figure 11 (c). Thus, we have established our base cases. Next, let n≥10𝑛10n\geq 10italic_n ≥ 10 and assume that if G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is a connected, cubic, and claw-free graph of order n′superscript𝑛′n^{\prime}italic_n start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, where 6≤n′<n6superscript𝑛′𝑛6\leq n^{\prime}<n6 ≤ italic_n start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT < italic_n, then Z⁢(G′)≤γ⁢(G′)+2𝑍superscript𝐺′𝛾superscript𝐺′2Z(G^{\prime})\leq\gamma(G^{\prime})+2italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) ≤ italic_γ ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 2.

Let G𝐺Gitalic_G be a connected, cubic, and claw-free graph with order n𝑛nitalic_n. If G∈𝒩cubic∗𝐺superscriptsubscript𝒩cubicG\in\mathcal{N}_{\text{cubic}}^{*}italic_G ∈ caligraphic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT cubic end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, then by Lemma 6, Z⁢(G)=γ⁢(G)+2𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺2Z(G)=\gamma(G)+2italic_Z ( italic_G ) = italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2. Thus, we may assume that G∉𝒩cubic∗𝐺superscriptsubscript𝒩cubicG\notin\mathcal{N}_{\text{cubic}}^{*}italic_G ∉ caligraphic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT cubic end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, since otherwise the desired result follows. Hence, at least one unit in the ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition of V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) is a diamond-unit. Since every triangle-unit of G𝐺Gitalic_G is joined by three edges to vertices in other units, and since every diamond-unit is joined by two edges to vertices in other units, it must be the case that there are at least two triangle-units in our ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition. If V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) does not contain a diamond-unit in its ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition, then G𝐺Gitalic_G has a spanning 2-factor consisting of triangles, which by Lemma 7, implies Z⁢(G)≤γ⁢(G)+1<γ⁢(G)+2𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺1𝛾𝐺2Z(G)\leq\gamma(G)+1<\gamma(G)+2italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 1 < italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2. Thus, we may further assume G𝐺Gitalic_G contains at least one diamond since otherwise, the desired result follows. Let D𝐷Ditalic_D be an arbitrary diamond-unit in the ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition of V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) and denote the vertices of D𝐷Ditalic_D by V⁢(D)={a,b,c,d}𝑉𝐷𝑎𝑏𝑐𝑑V(D)=\{a,b,c,d\}italic_V ( italic_D ) = { italic_a , italic_b , italic_c , italic_d }, where a⁢b𝑎𝑏abitalic_a italic_b is the missing edge in D𝐷Ditalic_D. Let e𝑒eitalic_e be the neighbor of a𝑎aitalic_a, not in D𝐷Ditalic_D, and f𝑓fitalic_f be the neighbor of b𝑏bitalic_b, not in f𝑓fitalic_f, where e≠f𝑒𝑓e\neq fitalic_e ≠ italic_f because G𝐺Gitalic_G is claw-free. The vertices e𝑒eitalic_e and f𝑓fitalic_f may, or may not be adjacent, and we consider these cases with the following claims.

Claim 7.

If e𝑒eitalic_e and f𝑓fitalic_f are not adjacent, then Z⁢(G)<γ⁢(G)+2𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺2Z(G)<\gamma(G)+2italic_Z ( italic_G ) < italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2.

Proof.

Suppose that e𝑒eitalic_e and f𝑓fitalic_f are not adjacent. Next, let e1subscript𝑒1e_{1}italic_e start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and e2subscript𝑒2e_{2}italic_e start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be the neighbors of e𝑒eitalic_e different from a𝑎aitalic_a, and let f1subscript𝑓1f_{1}italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and f2subscript𝑓2f_{2}italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be the neighbors of f𝑓fitalic_f different from b𝑏bitalic_b. Since G𝐺Gitalic_G is claw-free, note that {e,e1,e2}𝑒subscript𝑒1subscript𝑒2\{e,e_{1},e_{2}\}{ italic_e , italic_e start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_e start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } and {f,f1,f2}𝑓subscript𝑓1subscript𝑓2\{f,f_{1},f_{2}\}{ italic_f , italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } both induce triangles in G𝐺Gitalic_G, say Tesubscript𝑇𝑒T_{e}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and Tfsubscript𝑇𝑓T_{f}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_f end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, respectively. Note that Tesubscript𝑇𝑒T_{e}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and Tfsubscript𝑇𝑓T_{f}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_f end_POSTSUBSCRIPT share no common vertex. Moreover, Tesubscript𝑇𝑒T_{e}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_e end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and Tfsubscript𝑇𝑓T_{f}italic_T start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_f end_POSTSUBSCRIPT are themselves either triangle-units, or are a part of some diamond-unit in the ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition of V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ); see Figure 12 for an illustration of this configuration.

Let G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT be the graph obtained from G𝐺Gitalic_G by deleting the vertices in V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) and any edges incident edges from V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ), and then adding the edge e⁢f𝑒𝑓efitalic_e italic_f. Note that G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is a connected, cubic, and claw-free graph with order n′superscript𝑛′n^{\prime}italic_n start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, where 6≤n′<n6superscript𝑛′𝑛6\leq n^{\prime}<n6 ≤ italic_n start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT < italic_n. If the ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition of V⁢(G′)𝑉superscript𝐺′V(G^{\prime})italic_V ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) consists of only diamond-units and no triangle-units, then G∈𝒩cubic∗𝐺superscriptsubscript𝒩cubicG\in\mathcal{N}_{\text{cubic}}^{*}italic_G ∈ caligraphic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT cubic end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, a contradiction our earlier assumption that G∉𝒩cubic∗𝐺superscriptsubscript𝒩cubicG\notin\mathcal{N}_{\text{cubic}}^{*}italic_G ∉ caligraphic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT cubic end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. Thus, G′∉𝒩cubicsuperscript𝐺′subscript𝒩cubicG^{\prime}\notin\mathcal{N}_{\text{cubic}}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∉ caligraphic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT cubic end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Hence, the ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition of V⁢(G′)𝑉superscript𝐺′V(G^{\prime})italic_V ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) contains at least one triangle-unit. If G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is the prism C3⁢□⁢K2subscript𝐶3□subscript𝐾2C_{3}\>\Box\>K_{2}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 3 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT □ italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, then renaming vertices if necessary, we may assume eisubscript𝑒𝑖e_{i}italic_e start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and fisubscript𝑓𝑖f_{i}italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i end_POSTSUBSCRIPT are adjacent for i∈[2]𝑖delimited-[]2i\in[2]italic_i ∈ [ 2 ], which implies that G𝐺Gitalic_G is the graph with order n=10𝑛10n=10italic_n = 10 shown in Figure 13. The set {c,e,f1}𝑐𝑒subscript𝑓1\{c,e,f_{1}\}{ italic_c , italic_e , italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } is a minimum dominating set of G𝐺Gitalic_G, and the set {c,e,e1,f}𝑐𝑒subscript𝑒1𝑓\{c,e,e_{1},f\}{ italic_c , italic_e , italic_e start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , italic_f } is a minimum zero forcing set of G𝐺Gitalic_G. Thus, Z⁢(G)=4<γ⁢(G)+2=5𝑍𝐺4𝛾𝐺25Z(G)=4<\gamma(G)+2=5italic_Z ( italic_G ) = 4 < italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2 = 5. Hence, we may assume that G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is not the prism C3⁢□⁢K2subscript𝐶3□subscript𝐾2C_{3}\>\Box\>K_{2}italic_C start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 3 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT □ italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT since otherwise, our desired result follows. We proceed by proving the following two subclaims.

Claim 7.1.

Z⁢(G)=Z⁢(G′)+1𝑍𝐺𝑍superscript𝐺′1Z(G)=Z(G^{\prime})+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) = italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 1

Proof.

Let G𝐺Gitalic_G be a cubic and claw-free graph. Next, let D𝐷Ditalic_D be an arbitrary diamond-unit in the ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition of V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ) and denote the vertices of D𝐷Ditalic_D by V⁢(D)={a,b,c,d}𝑉𝐷𝑎𝑏𝑐𝑑V(D)=\{a,b,c,d\}italic_V ( italic_D ) = { italic_a , italic_b , italic_c , italic_d }, where a⁢b𝑎𝑏abitalic_a italic_b is the missing edge in D𝐷Ditalic_D. Let e𝑒eitalic_e be the neighbor of a𝑎aitalic_a, not in D𝐷Ditalic_D, and f𝑓fitalic_f be the neighbor of b𝑏bitalic_b, not in f𝑓fitalic_f, where e≠f𝑒𝑓e\neq fitalic_e ≠ italic_f because G𝐺Gitalic_G is claw-free.Let S′⊆V⁢(G′)superscript𝑆′𝑉superscript𝐺′S^{\prime}\subseteq V(G^{\prime})italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ⊆ italic_V ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) be a minimum zero forcing set of G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. If f∉S′𝑓superscript𝑆′f\notin S^{\prime}italic_f ∉ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, then at some iteration of the zero forcing process in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, a neighbor of f𝑓fitalic_f forces f𝑓fitalic_f to become colored blue. If f𝑓fitalic_f was forced to become colored blue during the zero forcing process in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, let S=S′∪{c}𝑆superscript𝑆′𝑐S=S^{\prime}\cup\{c\}italic_S = italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∪ { italic_c } in G𝐺Gitalic_G. If e𝑒eitalic_e was the vertex that forced f𝑓fitalic_f to become colored blue in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, starting from the blue coloring S𝑆Sitalic_S in G𝐺Gitalic_G, at some iteration of the zero forcing process e𝑒eitalic_e would force a𝑎aitalic_a to become colored blue. After a𝑎aitalic_a becomes colored blue, d𝑑ditalic_d would be the only white-colored neighbor of a𝑎aitalic_a, so a𝑎aitalic_a would then force d𝑑ditalic_d to become colored blue. After d𝑑ditalic_d becomes colored blue, b𝑏bitalic_b would be the only white-colored neighbor of d𝑑ditalic_d, so d𝑑ditalic_d would then force b𝑏bitalic_b to become colored blue. Finally, after b𝑏bitalic_b becomes colored blue, f𝑓fitalic_f would be the only white-colored neighbor of f𝑓fitalic_f, so d𝑑ditalic_d would force f𝑓fitalic_f to become colored blue. After f𝑓fitalic_f becomes colored blue, all color changes in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT starting from S′superscript𝑆′S^{\prime}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT would now occur in G𝐺Gitalic_G. Thus, S𝑆Sitalic_S is a zero forcing set of G𝐺Gitalic_G. Hence, Z⁢(G)≤|S′|+1=Z⁢(G′)+1𝑍𝐺superscript𝑆′1𝑍superscript𝐺′1Z(G)\leq|S^{\prime}|+1=Z(G^{\prime})+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ | italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | + 1 = italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 1. However, since every diamond-unit contains at least one vertex from every zero forcing set of G𝐺Gitalic_G, it must be the case that Z⁢(G)=Z⁢(G′)+1𝑍𝐺𝑍superscript𝐺′1Z(G)=Z(G^{\prime})+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) = italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 1.

Next, suppose that f𝑓fitalic_f was forced to become colored blue by either f1subscript𝑓1f_{1}italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT or f2subscript𝑓2f_{2}italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT. Without loss of generality, suppose f2subscript𝑓2f_{2}italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT forces f𝑓fitalic_f to become colored blue. At some iteration of the zero forcing process in G𝐺Gitalic_G f𝑓fitalic_f is the only white-colored neighbor of f2subscript𝑓2f_{2}italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, so f1subscript𝑓1f_{1}italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT was colored blue before f𝑓fitalic_f was colored blue. These color changes would still occur in G𝐺Gitalic_G starting from the set S𝑆Sitalic_S, after which the only white-colored neighbor of f𝑓fitalic_f would be b𝑏bitalic_b, and so, f𝑓fitalic_f may then force b𝑏bitalic_b to become colored blue. After b𝑏bitalic_b becomes colored blue, d𝑑ditalic_d would be the only white-colored neighbor, so b𝑏bitalic_b would force d𝑑ditalic_d to become colored blue. After d𝑑ditalic_d becomes colored blue, a𝑎aitalic_a would be the only white-colored neighbor of d𝑑ditalic_d, so d𝑑ditalic_d would then force a𝑎aitalic_a to become colored blue. At this point of the zero forcing process, all colors that happened in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT would still happen in G𝐺Gitalic_G. Thus, S𝑆Sitalic_S is a zero forcing set of G𝐺Gitalic_G. Hence, Z⁢(G)≤|S′|+1=Z⁢(G′)+1𝑍𝐺superscript𝑆′1𝑍superscript𝐺′1Z(G)\leq|S^{\prime}|+1=Z(G^{\prime})+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ | italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | + 1 = italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 1. However, since every diamond-unit contains at least one vertex from every zero forcing set of G𝐺Gitalic_G, it must be the case that Z⁢(G)=Z⁢(G′)+1𝑍𝐺𝑍superscript𝐺′1Z(G)=Z(G^{\prime})+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) = italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 1. Hence, the result follows if f𝑓fitalic_f was forced to become colored blue during the zero forcing process on G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT.

Since the above arguments apply to the vertex e𝑒eitalic_e, we may assume that neither e𝑒eitalic_e nor f𝑓fitalic_f become colored blue during the zero forcing process in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. In particular, this implies e,f∈S′𝑒𝑓superscript𝑆′e,f\in S^{\prime}italic_e , italic_f ∈ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. Next suppose that at some iteration of the zero forcing process in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, f2subscript𝑓2f_{2}italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT becomes colored blue. After f2subscript𝑓2f_{2}italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT becomes colored blue, the only white-colored neighbor of f2subscript𝑓2f_{2}italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT would be f1subscript𝑓1f_{1}italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, and so, f2subscript𝑓2f_{2}italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT may then force f1subscript𝑓1f_{1}italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become colored blue. After these color changes have in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT and G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, the only white-colored neighbor of f𝑓fitalic_f in G𝐺Gitalic_G would be b𝑏bitalic_b, so, f𝑓fitalic_f could then force b𝑏bitalic_b to become colored blue. After b𝑏bitalic_b becomes colored blue, d𝑑ditalic_d would be the only white-colored neighbor, so b𝑏bitalic_b would force d𝑑ditalic_d to become colored blue. After d𝑑ditalic_d becomes colored blue, a𝑎aitalic_a would be the only white-colored neighbor of d𝑑ditalic_d, so d𝑑ditalic_d would then force a𝑎aitalic_a to become colored blue. At this point of the zero forcing process, all colors that happened in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT would still happen in G𝐺Gitalic_G. Thus, S𝑆Sitalic_S is a zero forcing set of G𝐺Gitalic_G. Hence, Z⁢(G)≤|S′|+1=Z⁢(G′)+1𝑍𝐺superscript𝑆′1𝑍superscript𝐺′1Z(G)\leq|S^{\prime}|+1=Z(G^{\prime})+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ | italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | + 1 = italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 1. However, since every diamond-unit contains at least one vertex from every zero forcing set of G𝐺Gitalic_G, it must be the case that Z⁢(G)=Z⁢(G′)+1𝑍𝐺𝑍superscript𝐺′1Z(G)=Z(G^{\prime})+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) = italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 1. Hence, Z⁢(G)=Z⁢(G′)+1𝑍𝐺𝑍superscript𝐺′1Z(G)=Z(G^{\prime})+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) = italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 1 when any neighbor of f𝑓fitalic_f is forced to become colored blue by a vertex different than f𝑓fitalic_f during the zero forcing process on G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT.

Since the above arguments on f𝑓fitalic_f also apply to the vertex e𝑒eitalic_e, we may also assume that no neighbor of e𝑒eitalic_e is forced to become colored blue by a vertex different than e𝑒eitalic_e. Our suppositions combined imply that e𝑒eitalic_e and f𝑓fitalic_f each have exactly one neighbor, which is not contained in S′superscript𝑆′S^{\prime}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, where these white-colored neighbors are forced to become colored blue at the start of the zero forcing process in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. Without loss of generality, suppose e2subscript𝑒2e_{2}italic_e start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and f2subscript𝑓2f_{2}italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT are initially blue colored in S′superscript𝑆′S^{\prime}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, so e𝑒eitalic_e forces e1subscript𝑒1e_{1}italic_e start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become colored blue and f𝑓fitalic_f forces f1subscript𝑓1f_{1}italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT to become colored blue. Next let S′′=(S′∖{e})∪{f1}superscript𝑆′′superscript𝑆′𝑒subscript𝑓1S^{\prime\prime}=(S^{\prime}\setminus\{e\})\cup\{f_{1}\}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = ( italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∖ { italic_e } ) ∪ { italic_f start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT } be a different set of initially blue colored vertices in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, and then let S=S′′∪{c}𝑆superscript𝑆′′𝑐S=S^{\prime\prime}\cup\{c\}italic_S = italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∪ { italic_c } and note |S′′|=|S′|=Z⁢(G)superscript𝑆′′superscript𝑆′𝑍𝐺|S^{\prime\prime}|=|S^{\prime}|=Z(G)| italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | = | italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | = italic_Z ( italic_G ). Under the new coloring S𝑆Sitalic_S in G𝐺Gitalic_G, observe that the only white-colored neighbor of f𝑓fitalic_f in G𝐺Gitalic_G is b𝑏bitalic_b, and the only white-colored neighbor of f𝑓fitalic_f in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is e𝑒eitalic_e. Thus, f𝑓fitalic_f may force b𝑏bitalic_b to become colored blue in G𝐺Gitalic_G and e𝑒eitalic_e to become colored blue in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. Thus, S′′superscript𝑆′′S^{\prime\prime}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is a zero forcing set, implying |S′′|=Z⁢(G′)superscript𝑆′′𝑍superscript𝐺′|S^{\prime\prime}|=Z(G^{\prime})| italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | = italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ). Next, observe that after b𝑏bitalic_b becomes colored blue in G𝐺Gitalic_G, the only white-colored neighbor of b𝑏bitalic_b would be d𝑑ditalic_d, and so, b𝑏bitalic_b would force d𝑑ditalic_d to become colored blue. After d𝑑ditalic_d becomes colored blue, a𝑎aitalic_a would be the only white-colored neighbor of d𝑑ditalic_d, so d𝑑ditalic_d would then force a𝑎aitalic_a to become colored blue. After a𝑎aitalic_a becomes colored blue, e𝑒eitalic_e would be the only white-colored neighbor of a𝑎aitalic_a, so a𝑎aitalic_a may force e𝑒eitalic_e to become colored blue. After e𝑒eitalic_e becomes colored blue in G𝐺Gitalic_G, all color changes in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT will now occur in G𝐺Gitalic_G. Thus, S𝑆Sitalic_S is a zero forcing set of G𝐺Gitalic_G. Hence, Z⁢(G)≤|S′|+1=Z⁢(G′)+1𝑍𝐺superscript𝑆′1𝑍superscript𝐺′1Z(G)\leq|S^{\prime}|+1=Z(G^{\prime})+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ | italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | + 1 = italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 1. However, since every diamond-unit contains at least one vertex from every zero forcing set of G𝐺Gitalic_G, it must be the case that Z⁢(G)=Z⁢(G′)+1𝑍𝐺𝑍superscript𝐺′1Z(G)=Z(G^{\prime})+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) = italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 1.

Claim 7.2.

γ⁢(G)=γ⁢(G′)+1𝛾𝐺𝛾superscript𝐺′1\gamma(G)=\gamma(G^{\prime})+1italic_γ ( italic_G ) = italic_γ ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 1

Proof.

Let X′⊆V⁢(G′)superscript𝑋′𝑉superscript𝐺′X^{\prime}\subseteq V(G^{\prime})italic_X start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ⊆ italic_V ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) be a minimum (independent) dominating set of G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. If both e𝑒eitalic_e and f𝑓fitalic_f are not in X′superscript𝑋′X^{\prime}italic_X start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, then clearly X=X′∪{c}𝑋superscript𝑋′𝑐X=X^{\prime}\cup\{c\}italic_X = italic_X start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∪ { italic_c } is a minimum (independent) dominating set of G𝐺Gitalic_G implying γ⁢(G)=γ⁢(G′)+1𝛾𝐺𝛾superscript𝐺′1\gamma(G)=\gamma(G^{\prime})+1italic_γ ( italic_G ) = italic_γ ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 1. Thus, we will X′superscript𝑋′X^{\prime}italic_X start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT contains either e𝑒eitalic_e or f𝑓fitalic_f. Note that since e𝑒eitalic_e and f𝑓fitalic_f are adjacent in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, and since X′superscript𝑋′X^{\prime}italic_X start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT is independent, either e∈X′𝑒superscript𝑋′e\in X^{\prime}italic_e ∈ italic_X start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT and f∉X′𝑓superscript𝑋′f\notin X^{\prime}italic_f ∉ italic_X start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, or e∉X′𝑒superscript𝑋′e\notin X^{\prime}italic_e ∉ italic_X start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT and f∈X′𝑓superscript𝑋′f\in X^{\prime}italic_f ∈ italic_X start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. Without loss of generality, suppose e∉X′𝑒superscript𝑋′e\notin X^{\prime}italic_e ∉ italic_X start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT and f∈X′𝑓superscript𝑋′f\in X^{\prime}italic_f ∈ italic_X start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. Then, the set X=X′∪{a}𝑋superscript𝑋′𝑎X=X^{\prime}\cup\{a\}italic_X = italic_X start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∪ { italic_a } is clearly a minimum dominating set of G𝐺Gitalic_G. Thus, γ⁢(G)=γ⁢(G′)+1𝛾𝐺𝛾superscript𝐺′1\gamma(G)=\gamma(G^{\prime})+1italic_γ ( italic_G ) = italic_γ ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 1, completing out proof. (□□\Box□)

Apply the inductive hypothesis to the graph G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, and so,

Z⁢(G′)<γ⁢(G′)+2.𝑍superscript𝐺′𝛾superscript𝐺′2Z(G^{\prime})<\gamma(G^{\prime})+2.italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) < italic_γ ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 2 .

By Claim 7.1, Z⁢(G)=Z⁢(G′)+1𝑍𝐺𝑍superscript𝐺′1Z(G)=Z(G^{\prime})+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) = italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 1, and by Claim 7.2, γ⁢(G)=γ⁢(G′)+1𝛾𝐺𝛾superscript𝐺′1\gamma(G)=\gamma(G^{\prime})+1italic_γ ( italic_G ) = italic_γ ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 1. Thus,

Z⁢(G)−1=Z⁢(G′)<γ⁢(G′)+2=(γ⁢(G)−1)+2.𝑍𝐺1𝑍superscript𝐺′𝛾superscript𝐺′2𝛾𝐺12Z(G)-1=Z(G^{\prime})<\gamma(G^{\prime})+2=(\gamma(G)-1)+2.italic_Z ( italic_G ) - 1 = italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) < italic_γ ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 2 = ( italic_γ ( italic_G ) - 1 ) + 2 .

Hence,

Z⁢(G)<γ⁢(G)+2,𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺2Z(G)<\gamma(G)+2,italic_Z ( italic_G ) < italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2 ,

and the proof of our claim is finished. (□□\Box□)

By Claim 7, we may assume that e𝑒eitalic_e and f𝑓fitalic_f are adjacent in G𝐺Gitalic_G since otherwise, the desired inequality follows. Thus, e𝑒eitalic_e and f𝑓fitalic_f belong to a common triangle-unit in the ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition of V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ), say T𝑇Titalic_T. Let g𝑔gitalic_g be the remaining vertex in the triangle-unit T𝑇Titalic_T, and let hℎhitalic_h be the neighbor of g𝑔gitalic_g not in T𝑇Titalic_T. Further, let i𝑖iitalic_i and j𝑗jitalic_j be the two vertices in the triangle containing hℎhitalic_h. If hℎhitalic_h belongs to a diamond-unit, then choosing this diamond-unit initially in our argument would bring us back to Claim 7, implying Z⁢(G)≤γ⁢(G)+1𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺1Z(G)\leq\gamma(G)+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 1. Thus, we may assume that hℎhitalic_h belongs to a triangle-unit in the ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition of V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ); that is {h,i,j}ℎ𝑖𝑗\{h,i,j\}{ italic_h , italic_i , italic_j } form a triangle-unit. Let k𝑘kitalic_k and ℓℓ\ellroman_ℓ denote the neighbors of i𝑖iitalic_i and j𝑗jitalic_j, respectively, not contained in {h,i,j}ℎ𝑖𝑗\{h,i,j\}{ italic_h , italic_i , italic_j }. By assumption, the vertex hℎhitalic_h does not belong to a diamond-unit, so k≠ℓ𝑘ℓk\neq\ellitalic_k ≠ roman_ℓ. The resulting subgraph of G𝐺Gitalic_G is illustrated in Figure 14, where k𝑘kitalic_k and ℓℓ\ellroman_ℓ may be adjacent vertices.

Suppose now that the vertex k𝑘kitalic_k belongs to a diamond-unit D∗superscript𝐷D^{*}italic_D start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT in G𝐺Gitalic_G, which implies that D∗superscript𝐷D^{*}italic_D start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT also is a diamond-unit of G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT considered previously. If the diamond-unit D∗superscript𝐷D^{*}italic_D start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT does not contain the vertex ℓℓ\ellroman_ℓ, then choosing this diamond-unit as our initial diamond-unit D𝐷Ditalic_D brings us back to Claim 7, which implies that Z⁢(G)≤γ⁢(G)+2𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺2Z(G)\leq\gamma(G)+2italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2.

We will now assume that the diamond-unit D∗superscript𝐷D^{*}italic_D start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT contains the vertex ℓℓ\ellroman_ℓ. Thus, G𝐺Gitalic_G is a graph with order n=14𝑛14n=14italic_n = 14 shown in Figure 15, where V⁢(D∗)={k,ℓ,m,p}𝑉superscript𝐷𝑘ℓ𝑚𝑝V(D^{*})=\{k,\ell,m,p\}italic_V ( italic_D start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) = { italic_k , roman_ℓ , italic_m , italic_p }. Then, the set {a,c,e,i,m}𝑎𝑐𝑒𝑖𝑚\{a,c,e,i,m\}{ italic_a , italic_c , italic_e , italic_i , italic_m } is a minimum zero forcing set of G𝐺Gitalic_G, and the set {c,e,i,m}𝑐𝑒𝑖𝑚\{c,e,i,m\}{ italic_c , italic_e , italic_i , italic_m } is a minimum dominating set of G𝐺Gitalic_G. Thus,

Z⁢(G)=5<γ⁢(G)+2=7.𝑍𝐺5𝛾𝐺27Z(G)=5<\gamma(G)+2=7.italic_Z ( italic_G ) = 5 < italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2 = 7 .

Hence, we may assume that the vertex k𝑘kitalic_k belongs to a triangle-unit in the ΔΔ\Deltaroman_Δ-D-partition of V⁢(G)𝑉𝐺V(G)italic_V ( italic_G ), since otherwise the desired result follows.

Consider now the connected, cubic, and claw-free graph G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT with order n′superscript𝑛′n^{\prime}italic_n start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT obtained from G𝐺Gitalic_G by removing the vertices in the set {e,f,g,h,i,j}𝑒𝑓𝑔ℎ𝑖𝑗\{e,f,g,h,i,j\}{ italic_e , italic_f , italic_g , italic_h , italic_i , italic_j } and any edges incident with this set, and then adding the edges a⁢k𝑎𝑘akitalic_a italic_k and b⁢ℓ𝑏ℓb\ellitalic_b roman_ℓ.

Claim 8.

Z⁢(G)≤Z⁢(G′)+2𝑍𝐺𝑍superscript𝐺′2Z(G)\leq Z(G^{\prime})+2italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 2

Proof.

Let S′superscript𝑆′S^{\prime}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT be a minimum zero forcing set of G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT and let D𝐷Ditalic_D be the diamond-unit of G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT (and also of G𝐺Gitalic_G) with vertex set V⁢(D)={a,b,c,d}𝑉𝐷𝑎𝑏𝑐𝑑V(D)=\{a,b,c,d\}italic_V ( italic_D ) = { italic_a , italic_b , italic_c , italic_d }. We will consider the following three subclaims. Suppose first that |V⁢(D)∩S′|=3𝑉𝐷superscript𝑆′3|V(D)\cap S^{\prime}|=3| italic_V ( italic_D ) ∩ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | = 3. In this case Let S=S′∪{i}𝑆superscript𝑆′𝑖S=S^{\prime}\cup\{i\}italic_S = italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∪ { italic_i }. Note that any three vertices of D𝐷Ditalic_D will zero force all vertices of D𝐷Ditalic_D to become colored blue, and so, we assume S′∩D={a,b,c}superscript𝑆′𝐷𝑎𝑏𝑐S^{\prime}\cap D=\{a,b,c\}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∩ italic_D = { italic_a , italic_b , italic_c }. Then, as shown in Figure 16, all vertices of the subgraph H𝐻Hitalic_H in G𝐺Gitalic_G become colored blue. Then, after k𝑘kitalic_k and j𝑗jitalic_j become colored blue, all color changes in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT will happen in G𝐺Gitalic_G. Thus, S𝑆Sitalic_S is a zero forcing set of G𝐺Gitalic_G. Hence, Z⁢(G)≤|S|=|S′|+1=Z⁢(G′)+1<Z⁢(G′)+2𝑍𝐺𝑆superscript𝑆′1𝑍superscript𝐺′1𝑍superscript𝐺′2Z(G)\leq|S|=|S^{\prime}|+1=Z(G^{\prime})+1<Z(G^{\prime})+2italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ | italic_S | = | italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | + 1 = italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 1 < italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 2.

Next suppose |V⁢(D)∩S′|=2𝑉𝐷superscript𝑆′2|V(D)\cap S^{\prime}|=2| italic_V ( italic_D ) ∩ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | = 2. In this case, we may assume without loss of generality that a∉S′𝑎superscript𝑆′a\notin S^{\prime}italic_a ∉ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT and let S=S′∪{a,i}𝑆superscript𝑆′𝑎𝑖S=S^{\prime}\cup\{a,i\}italic_S = italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∪ { italic_a , italic_i }. Then, as shown in Figure 16, all vertices of the subgraph H𝐻Hitalic_H in G𝐺Gitalic_G become colored blue. Then, after k𝑘kitalic_k and j𝑗jitalic_j become colored blue, all color changes in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT will happen in G𝐺Gitalic_G. Thus, S𝑆Sitalic_S is a zero forcing set of G𝐺Gitalic_G. Hence, Z⁢(G)≤|S|=|S′|+2≤Z⁢(G′)+2𝑍𝐺𝑆superscript𝑆′2𝑍superscript𝐺′2Z(G)\leq|S|=|S^{\prime}|+2\leq Z(G^{\prime})+2italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ | italic_S | = | italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | + 2 ≤ italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 2.

Suppose |V⁢(D)∩S′|=1𝑉𝐷superscript𝑆′1|V(D)\cap S^{\prime}|=1| italic_V ( italic_D ) ∩ italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | = 1, and so, either c𝑐citalic_c or d𝑑ditalic_d belong to S′superscript𝑆′S^{\prime}italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. If Moreover, either a𝑎aitalic_a is forced to become colored blue by i𝑖iitalic_i in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, or b𝑏bitalic_b is forced to become colored blue by ℓℓ\ellroman_ℓ in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. Without loss of generality suppose ℓℓ\ellroman_ℓ forces b𝑏bitalic_b to become colored blue in G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. Under this assumption we let S=S′∪{e,i}𝑆superscript𝑆′𝑒𝑖S=S^{\prime}\cup\{e,i\}italic_S = italic_S start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∪ { italic_e , italic_i }. Then in G𝐺Gitalic_G, at some point of the zero forcing process starting with with S𝑆Sitalic_S as our initial set of blue colored vertices, ℓℓ\ellroman_ℓ would force j𝑗jitalic_j to become colored blue, thereafter, all vertices of H𝐻Hitalic_H become colored blue as shown in Figure 17. (□□\Box□)

Claim 9.

γ⁢(G′)≤γ⁢(G)−2𝛾superscript𝐺′𝛾𝐺2\gamma(G^{\prime})\leq\gamma(G)-2italic_γ ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) ≤ italic_γ ( italic_G ) - 2

Proof.

Observe there exists some minimum dominating set of G𝐺Gitalic_G containing the set of vertices {c,g,k,ℓ}𝑐𝑔𝑘ℓ\{c,g,k,\ell\}{ italic_c , italic_g , italic_k , roman_ℓ }. If X𝑋Xitalic_X is this dominating set, then the let X′=X∖{i,g}superscript𝑋′𝑋𝑖𝑔X^{\prime}=X\setminus\{i,g\}italic_X start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = italic_X ∖ { italic_i , italic_g } is a dominating set of G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. Thus,

γ⁢(G′)≤|X′|=|X|−2=γ⁢(G)−2.𝛾superscript𝐺′superscript𝑋′𝑋2𝛾𝐺2\gamma(G^{\prime})\leq|X^{\prime}|=|X|-2=\gamma(G)-2.italic_γ ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) ≤ | italic_X start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | = | italic_X | - 2 = italic_γ ( italic_G ) - 2 .

(□□\Box□)

Now that we have established our final claims, apply the inductive hypothesis to the graph G′superscript𝐺′G^{\prime}italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT, and observe

Z⁢(G′)<γ⁢(G′)+2.𝑍superscript𝐺′𝛾superscript𝐺′2Z(G^{\prime})<\gamma(G^{\prime})+2.italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) < italic_γ ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 2 .

Then by Claim 8, Z⁢(G)≤Z⁢(G′)+2𝑍𝐺𝑍superscript𝐺′2Z(G)\leq Z(G^{\prime})+2italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 2, and by Claim 9, γ⁢(G′)=γ⁢(G)−2𝛾superscript𝐺′𝛾𝐺2\gamma(G^{\prime})=\gamma(G)-2italic_γ ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) = italic_γ ( italic_G ) - 2. Thus,

Z⁢(G)−2=Z⁢(G′)<γ⁢(G′)+2≤(γ⁢(G)−2)+2.𝑍𝐺2𝑍superscript𝐺′𝛾superscript𝐺′2𝛾𝐺22Z(G)-2=Z(G^{\prime})<\gamma(G^{\prime})+2\leq(\gamma(G)-2)+2.italic_Z ( italic_G ) - 2 = italic_Z ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) < italic_γ ( italic_G start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ′ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ) + 2 ≤ ( italic_γ ( italic_G ) - 2 ) + 2 .

Hence,

Z⁢(G)<γ⁢(G)+2,𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺2Z(G)<\gamma(G)+2,italic_Z ( italic_G ) < italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2 ,

and the proof of the desired result is finished. ∎

4 Conclusion

In this paper, we have proven Z⁢(G)≤γ⁢(G)+2𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺2Z(G)\leq\gamma(G)+2italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2 for any connected, cubic, and claw-free graph G𝐺Gitalic_G. This result resolves Conjecture 1 in the affirmative and so provides further support for the usefulness of the artificial intelligence program TxGraffiti. Moreover, since γ⁢(G)≤α⁢(G)𝛾𝐺𝛼𝐺\gamma(G)\leq\alpha(G)italic_γ ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_α ( italic_G ) for all graphs, Theorem 8 also improves on the bound Z⁢(G)≤α⁢(G)+1𝑍𝐺𝛼𝐺1Z(G)\leq\alpha(G)+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_α ( italic_G ) + 1 for connected, cubic, and claw-free graphs given in [6, 10]. Indeed,

Z⁢(Nk)=γ⁢(Nk)+2<α⁢(Nk)+1,𝑍subscript𝑁𝑘𝛾subscript𝑁𝑘2𝛼subscript𝑁𝑘1Z(N_{k})=\gamma(N_{k})+2<\alpha(N_{k})+1,italic_Z ( italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) = italic_γ ( italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) + 2 < italic_α ( italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) + 1 ,

for all diamond-necklaces Nksubscript𝑁𝑘N_{k}italic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT with k≥3𝑘3k\geq 3italic_k ≥ 3. Furthermore, when the additional condition that G𝐺Gitalic_G is claw-free is imposed, Theorem 8 also improves on the more general bound Z⁢(G)≤2⁢γ⁢(G)𝑍𝐺2𝛾𝐺Z(G)\leq 2\gamma(G)italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ 2 italic_γ ( italic_G ) for any connected and cubic graph G𝐺Gitalic_G, other than K4subscript𝐾4K_{4}italic_K start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 4 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT, presented in [6, 9].

By Theorem 8, if G𝐺Gitalic_G is connected, cubic, and claw-free, then Z⁢(G)=γ⁢(G)+2𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺2Z(G)=\gamma(G)+2italic_Z ( italic_G ) = italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2, if and only if G∈𝒩cubic∗𝐺superscriptsubscript𝒩cubicG\in\mathcal{N}_{\text{cubic}}^{*}italic_G ∈ caligraphic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT cubic end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. It therefore seems interesting to find all connected, cubic, and claw-free graphs for which Z⁢(G)=γ⁢(G)+1𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺1Z(G)=\gamma(G)+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) = italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 1.

Question 1.

What connected, cubic, and claw-free graphs with G∉𝒩cubic∗𝐺superscriptsubscript𝒩cubicG\notin\mathcal{N}_{\text{cubic}}^{*}italic_G ∉ caligraphic_N start_POSTSUBSCRIPT cubic end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ∗ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT satisfy Z⁢(G)≤γ⁢(G)+1𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺1Z(G)\leq\gamma(G)+1italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 1 with equality?

Surprisingly, after completing the proof of Theorem 8, we discovered the following conjecture of TxGraffiti while prompting it to directly conjecture on the zero forcing number for all possible combinations of the hypothesis that included cubic graphs.

Conjecture 9 (TxGraffiti – Open).

If G𝐺Gitalic_G is a connected, cubic, and diamond-free graph, then

Z⁢(G)≤γ⁢(G)+2,𝑍𝐺𝛾𝐺2Z(G)\leq\gamma(G)+2,italic_Z ( italic_G ) ≤ italic_γ ( italic_G ) + 2 ,

and this bound is sharp.

Conjecture 9 was ranked as more substantial than the more well-known α𝛼\alphaitalic_α-Z Conjecture, so this conjecture also warrants further investigation.

References

  • [1] AIM Special Work Group, Zero forcing sets and the minimum rank of graphs, Linear Algebra Appl., 428(7) (2008), 1628–1648.
  • [2] R. B. Allan and R. Laskar, On domination and independent domination numbers of a graph, Discrete Math., 23(2) (1978), 73–76.
  • [3] B. Brimkov, R. Davila, H. Schuerger, and M. Young, On a conjecture of TxGraffiti: Relating zero forcing and vertex covers in graphs, Discrete Appl. Math., 359 (2023), 290–302.
  • [4] Y. Caro, R. Davila, M. A. Henning, and R. Pepper, Conjectures of TxGraffiti: Independence, domination, and matchings, Australas. J. Comb., 84(2) (2022), 258–274.
  • [5] Y. Caro, R. Davila, and R. Pepper, Independence, domination, and matchings, Discuss. Math. Graph Theory, 42(3) (2022), 921–935.
  • [6] R. Davila, Total and Zero Forcing in Graphs. University of Johannesburg, Ph.D Thesis, May 2019.
  • [7] R. Davila and M. A. Henning, Total forcing and zero forcing in claw-free cubic graphs, Graphs Combin., 34 (2018), 1371–1384.
  • [8] R. Davila and M. A. Henning, Total forcing versus total domination in cubic graphs, Appl. Math. Comput., 354 (2019), 385–395.
  • [9] R. Davila and M. A. Hennings, Relating zero forcing and domination in cubic graphs, J. Comb. Optim., 41 (2021), 553–577.
  • [10] R. Davila and M. A. Henning, Zero forcing in claw-free cubic graphs, Bull. Malays. Math. Sci. Soc., 43 (2020), 673–688.
  • [11] E. DeLaVina, Graffiti.pc: a variant of Graffiti, DIMACS Ser. Discret. Math. Theor. Comput. Sci., 69 (2005), p. 71.
  • [12] E. DeLaVina, Some history of the development of Graffiti, Graphs and Discovery, DIMACS Ser. Discret. Math. Theor. Comput. Sci., vol. 69, Amer. Math. Soc., Providence, RI (2005), 81–118.
  • [13] L. Hogben, J. C.-H. Lin, and B. L. Shader, Inverse Problems and Zero Forcing for Graphs (AMS Mathematical Surveys and Monographs, 270) . ISBN: 978-1-4704-6655-8 (2022).
  • [14] E. Flandrin, R. Faudree, and Z. Ryjáček, Claw-free graph - a survey, Discrete Math.,214 (2016), 196–200.
  • [15] T. Haynes, S. Hedetniemi, and M. A. Henning, Topics in Domination in Graphs (Springer Developments in Mathematics, 64). ISBN: 978-3-031-09495-8 (2021).
  • [16] T. Haynes, S. Hedetniemi, and M. A. Henning, Domination in Graphs: Core Concepts (Springer Monographs in Mathematics). ISBN: 978-3-031-09495-8 (2023).
  • [17] M. A. Henning and C. Löwenstein, Locating-total domination in claw-free cubic graphs. Discrete Math., 312(21) (2012), 3107–3116.
